items.article_text:
TracingArt
Millions of Resources, Spanning Five Centuries
Here's how the Getty Provenance Index is transforming research on the social life of art.
[Still Life with a Chinese Porcelain Jar](https://collections.discovernewfields.org/art/artwork/57562), Willem Kalf
Gift of Mrs. James W. Fesler in memory of Daniel W. and Elizabeth C. Marmon,
Indianapolis Museum of Art at Newfields, [No copyright](https://creativecommons.org/publicdomain/mark/1.0/)
The following year, Knoedler & Co. sold it to "Mrs. James W. Fesler," who donated it to the Indianapolis Museum of Art at Newfields the same year.
Records show her full name was Caroline Marmon Fesler.
Often disguised behind their husbands' names, female collectors played a major role in the formation of many American museum collections.
Studying an object's trajectory - from the moment it's created, then as it moves between new owners or custodians
and eventually is lost or destroyed, or reaches its present location - is the provenance researcher's job.
In 1669, Dutch artist Willem Kalf painted Still Life with a Chinese Porcelain Jar.
Kalf worked in 1600s Amsterdam, a dynamic trade center where luxurious imports - like the Chinese porcelain jar,
Venetian goblet, and Ottoman rug depicted in his painting - circulated widely, and were a sign of cosmopolitan taste.
For the next two centuries of its existence, it's hard to know much about how and where this painting traveled.
In 1864, it was sold at auction in Paris, but the name of the buyer remains unknown.
In 1936, it was part of an art exhibition in Amsterdam.
A label on the back of the painting shows it was lent by the art dealer Dr. H. Burg.
Sometime between 1936 and 1944, the painting traveled to New York with a couple from Cologne, Germany.
Ongoing [research](https://collections.discovernewfields.org/art/provenance-research) by the Indianapolis Museum of
Art at Newfields, where the painting is now,
seeks to clarify the movements of artworks during periods of conflict - such as World War II,
when many artworks were taken from their rightful owners by the Nazi regime.
In 1944, the famous art dealership Michael Knoedler & Co. purchased the painting.
1669
1700
1864
1936
1940
1944
The hundreds of thousands of transactions in
the newly reconfigured [Getty Provenance Index](https://www.getty.edu/research/provenance)
form a complex web of relationships between
objects, people, events, places and time.
Through it, we can see that painters acquired works from fellow artists, and often how much they paid for them.
In 1892, Claude Monet purchased
La Jatte de lait by Berthe Morisot at her first solo show. Both were active members of the social and artistic circles of the Impressionists in Paris.
Berthe Morisot
Claude Monet
[La Jatte de lait](https://www.sothebys.com/en/buy/auction/2022/modern-day-auction/la-jatte-de-lait-2), Berthe Morisot
Private Collection, Photograph Courtesy of Sotheby's, Inc. ©
Caravaggio
Giuseppe Cesari
[Boy with Basket of Fruit, Merisi Michelangelo](https://www.collezionegalleriaborghese.it/opere/giovane-con-canestra-di-frutta), called Caravaggio
Galleria Borghese, © Galleria Borghese / ph. Mauro Coen
[Self-Portrait as Bacchus (known as "Sick Bacchus")](https://www.collezionegalleriaborghese.it/en/opere/self-portrait-as-bacchus-known-as-sick-bacchus), Merisi Michelangelo, called Caravaggio
Galleria Borghese, © Galleria Borghese / ph. Mauro Coen
300 years earlier in Rome, artist Giuseppe Cesari was Caravaggio's first teacher, and obtained two of the artist's most famous paintings.
The paintings and others were seized- perhaps on trumped up charges- by Cardinal Scipione Borghese in 1607, and still reside in the Galleria Borghese to this day.
Paulus Potter
Adolphe Schloss
Jean-Baptiste Pierre Lebrun
Chevalier Lambert
André Coquille
William Norton
Baron Hastings
Charles Sedelmeyer
Years later, the painting was looted by the Vichy Government of France during WW2
[The "Piebald" Horse](https://www.getty.edu/art/collection/object

May 7, 2026

[Product](https://openai.com/index/advancing-voice-intelligence-with-new-models-in-the-api/news/product-releases/)[Release](https://openai.com/index/advancing-voice-intelligence-with-new-models-in-the-api/research/index/release/)

# Advancing voice intelligence with new models in the API

A new generation of realtime voice models that can reason, translate, and transcribe as people speak.

00:0004:03

Listen to article

8:31

Share

We’re introducing three audio models in the API that unlock a new class of voice apps for developers. With these models, developers can build voice experiences that feel more natural, respond more intelligently, and take action in real time:

* **GPT‑Realtime‑2**, our first voice model with GPT‑5‑class reasoning that can handle harder requests and carry the conversation forward naturally.
* **GPT‑Realtime‑Translate**, a new live translation model that translates speech from 70+ input languages into 13 output languages while keeping pace with the speaker.
* **GPT‑Realtime‑Whisper**, a new streaming speech-to-text that transcribes speech live as the speaker talks.

# Try GPT-Realtime-2

Start the session, then talk naturally with GPT-Realtime-2.

Start sessionWhat can I ask?

After you start the session, try saying one of these:

* I’m hosting a last-minute dinner tonight. I have 30 minutes, two vegetarian friends, one mushroom-hater, and a tiny kitchen. Help me plan a simple menu.
* I’m welcoming guests to a live event in Japan. Say a warm, natural welcome in Japanese — like a host kicking off something special.
* My order number is Orbit-742Q. Repeat it back clearly so I can confirm it’s right.
* Help me practice telling my team we hit our launch milestone. First say it with quiet confidence, then with more excitement.
* I’m planning trivia for a road trip. Give me three trick questions that sound deceivingly simple, then explain each answer in one sentence.

This demo is time-limited. By using it, you agree to OpenAI's [Terms](https://openai.com/index/advancing-voice-intelligence-with-new-models-in-the-api/policies/terms-of-use/) and acknowledge our [Privacy Policy](https://openai.com/index/advancing-voice-intelligence-with-new-models-in-the-api/policies/privacy-policy/).

## 

Voice is becoming one of the most natural ways for people to use software. It lets someone ask for help while driving, change a travel plan while walking through an airport, get support in their preferred language, or move through a task without

items.article_text:
TL;DR: Rolldown, the high-performance Bundler for JavaScript, reached 1.0 stable and production readiness. It features speed, Rollup plugin compatibility, and unique bundler features. Upgrading from the latest RC does not need any code changes.
Rolldown reaches 1.0 [](https://voidzero.dev#rolldown-reaches-1-0)
Rolldown is a Rust-based high-performance JavaScript bundler. It can be used standalone and is powering Vite since its latest major version Vite 8. Now Rolldown itself hits the 1.0 stable milestone.
Rolldown adheres to semantic versioning meaing the ^1.0.0
API is now locked:
- Option names, types, and plugin hook signatures stay backward-compatible.
- No planned breaking changes to Rolldown's public API.
Except for features marked [experimental](https://rolldown.rs/reference/InputOptions.experimental).
Output behavior may still change
Output behavior like Dead Code Elimination, chunking, and inlining heuristics will keep improving, and option defaults may shift to deliver smaller and faster bundles. This does NOT change the runtime behavior of the output. Behavioral changes are noted in release notes.
Built with Vite in mind [](https://voidzero.dev#built-with-vite-in-mind)
One of Rolldown's main goals was to unify Vite's original 2-bundler setup. The previous configuration worked, but it meant two transformation pipelines, two plugin systems, and a growing layer of glue code. It also limited Vite's future capabilities.
Rolldown solved this as a bundler by combining the best parts of existing bundlers. esbuild's speed and Rollup's plugin API. In addition, the bundler provides features neither had. (More on this below)
Swapping Vite's bundler was a 2 year journey. How we got here:
- April 2024: First public
0.10.1
release. - December 2024:
1.0.0-beta.1
, scoping the eventual 1.0 surface area. - May 2025:
, a temporary package, shipped as a technical preview to prepare for the swap and gather feedback from the community.rolldown-vite
- December 2025:
[Vite 8 beta](https://voidzero.dev/posts/announcing-vite-8-beta)shipped with Rolldown as the default bundler. - January 2026:
[Rolldown 1.0 RC](https://voidzero.dev/posts/announcing-rolldown-rc)marked API stability. - March 2026:
[Vite 8 stable](https://vite.dev/blog/announcing-vite8)shipped, making Rolldown the underlying bundler for every Vite user. - May 2026: Rolldown 1.0 stable.
Vite 8 is now Rolldown's main entry point. Meaning new Rolldown features and improvements directly benefit Vite users.
Why switch to Rolldown [](https://voidzero.dev#why-switch-to-rolldown)
Rolldown is a native bundler that combines speed and flexibility through it's plugin API. On top of that, it has unique features that improve the bundling experience and output quality.
Companies like [Framer](https://voidzero.dev/posts/case-study-framer-rolldown) and [PLAID](https://voidzero.dev/posts/case-study-plaid-rolldown) are already using Rolldown in production.
esbuild-like speed [](https://voidzero.dev#esbuild-like-speed)
Rolldown is 10x-30x faster than Rollup (the gap widens as project size grows) and on par with esbuild. Performance is a feature. Rolldown is written in Rust and leverages [Oxc](https://oxc.rs/) to handle the language work like parsing and minification.
Highlights:
- Ramp reduced their build time by 57%
- Mercedes-Benz.io cut their build time down by up to 38%
- Beehiiv reduced their build time by 64%
Full benchmarks: [ rolldown/benchmarks](https://github.com/rolldown/benchmarks).
Rollup plugin-compatibility [](https://voidzero.dev#rollup-plugin-compatibility)
Rolldown needed a Rollup-compatible plugin API to be a drop-in replacement for Vite. Otherwise, developers would have to make massive code changes to migrate to the latest Vite, which is a terrible experience. It also ensures users can continue using their existing plugins. Most Vite plugins work out of the box on Vite 8.
With Rolldown being written in native Rust, there was performance improvement opportunities in the plugin API

items.article_text:
The World Inside Neural Networks
How neural geometry will unlock understanding and control of AI
Authors
Correspondence to atticus@goodfire.ai, ekdeep@goodfire.ai
Published
May 7, 2026
Our world is full of objects and richly structured geometric relationships—the color wheel, road networks, family trees, months of the year—and humans use structured mental concepts to represent and navigate this complex reality[1][Seung, 1999](https://www.pnas.org/doi/10.1073/pnas.93.23.13339), [Behrens et al., 2018](https://www.cell.com/neuron/fulltext/S0896-6273(18)30856-0), [Sohn et al., 2019](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31320220/), [Chung & Abbott, 2021](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0959438821001227?via%3Dihub), [Kriegeskorte & Wei, 2021](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41583-021-00502-3), [Khona & Fiete, 2022](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41583-022-00642-0).
We also know that the inner world of neural networks is full of structure that reflects the structure of the outer world. This is true across models, modalities, and domains: In the internal representations of language models, numbers[2][Nanda et al. 2023](https://arxiv.org/abs/2301.05217), [Zhong et al. 2023](https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.17844), [Zhou et al. 2024](https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2024/hash/2cc8dc30e52798b27d37b795cc153310-Abstract-Conference.html), [Zhou et al. 2025](https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.09741), [Kantamneni and Tegmark 2025](https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.00873), [Levy and Geva 2025](https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.11781), [Fu et al. 2026](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.20817), days of the week, and months of the year[3][Engels et al. 2024](https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.14860), [Modell et al. 2025](https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.18235), [Karkada et al. 2026](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.15029), [Prieto et al. 2026](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.09972) are circular loops, and smooth curves represent the years in history[4][Engels et al. 2024](https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.14860), [Modell et al. 2025](https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.18235) and characters in a line of text[5][Gurnee et al. 2025](https://transformer-circuits.pub/2025/linebreaks/index.html). In image models, objects' spatial arrangement is recapitulated in activation space[6][Fel et al. 2025a](https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.08638) and colors are represented on smooth surfaces structured by hue, saturation, and lightness[7][Flachot et al. 2022](https://jov.arvojournals.org/article.aspx?articleid=2778712), [Pach et al. 2026](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.12261), [Yeh et al. 2025](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/10297591). In a genomic model, the entire tree of life lies on a complex manifold[8][Pearce et al. 2025](https://www.goodfire.ai/research/phylogeny-manifold), and we discovered a previously-unknown class of Alzheimer's biomarkers as a clean curve in an epigenomic model[9][Wang et al. 2026](https://www.goodfire.ai/research/interpretability-for-alzheimers-detection). At this point, the evidence for curved geometric structure inside neural networks is abundant and undeniable[10][Kozlowski et al. 2018](https://arxiv.org/abs/1803.09288), [Chung et al. 2018](https://journals.aps.org/prx/abstract/10.1103/PhysRevX.8.031003), [Saxe et al. 2019](https://www.pnas.org/doi/abs/10.1073/pnas.1820226116), [Cohen et al. 2020](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-020-14578-5), [Chung and Abbott 2021](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0959438821001227), [Abduo et al. 2021](https://aclanthology.org/2021.conll-1.9/), [Black et al. 2022](https://arxiv.org/abs/2211.12312), [Park et al. 2024](https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.00070), [Csordas et al. 2024](https://aclanthology.org/2024.blackboxnlp-1.17.pdf), [Diego-Simon et al. 2024](https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.05571), [Gorton 2024](https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.03662), [Marchetti et al. 2024](https://proceedings.mlr.press/v247/marchetti24a/marchetti24a.pdf), [Bethune et al. 2025](https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.18230), [Dooms and Gauderis 2025](https://arx

items.article_text: Product OS Pricing Docs Community Company More Get started – free 1 home.mdx Product OS Pricing customers.mdx demo.mov Docs Talk to a human Ask a question Sign up ↗ Switch to website mode Why PostHog? Changelog Company handbook Store Work here Trash Zoom Font Share Discord The era of self-driving development is here PostHog Code is the only AI devtool that understands your product, not just your codebase . Identifies product usage patterns Triages bugs and errors Creates pull requests automatically Join the waitlist Test drives begin Spring 2026 The old way to build with AI Coding with AI (cir. 2022-2025) 1. Analyze usage Human 2. Decide what to build Human 3. Prompt & context Human 4. Build Machine 5. Ship Human 1. Analyze usage 2. Decide what to build 3. Prompt & context 4. Build 5. Ship → → → → Human Human Human Machine Human Most AI code editors lack context and wait for you to tell them what to Build ↵ . Coding with AI (cir. 2022-2025) 1. Analyze usage Human 2. Decide what to build Human 3. Prompt & context Human 4. Build Machine 5. Ship Human 1. Analyze usage 2. Decide what to build 3. Prompt & context 4. Build 5. Ship → → → → Human Human Human Machine Human They use your <codebase /> as the source of truth, not how humans (or agents) actually use your product . The PostHog way Signals In-app activity Logs Errors Payments Session recordings Traces Funnel dropoff Feature flags Experiment results Internal threads Support tickets Backlog Transcriptions PostHog Code uses signals from ● production data to diagnose issues and generate pull requests to fix them – before you even know there's a problem. Run it locally or in the cloud – either way, it automatically uses the right AI model for the job. TL;DR: There are plenty of AI coding tools, but only one that knows your product like PostHog Code . Everything you'd expect in an AI coding tool, but way more... Command center Signals inbox Plan Review Skills Manage multiple coding agents in parallel Split screen presets let you monitor agents side-by-side or in a 2x2 or 3x3 grid. Automatic PostHog instrumentation PostHog Code automatically builds new features with the proper PostHog instrumentation so you can progressively roll out changes and monitor their impact. Event instrumentation Tracks new features and changes to existing features as they're built Error tracking Configures exception capture so new code surfaces in PostHog with stack traces Feature flags & rollout conditions Creates the flag, implements isFeatureEnabled , and configures staged rollout conditions Experiments Scaffolds variants, split, and goal metrics Supported LLMs PostHog Code runs on the same models you already use – no markup. OpenAI GPT-5.5 GPT-5.4 Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.6 Claude Opus 4.7 Claude Haiku 4.5 Integrations Linear Slack GitHub Granola Zendesk MCP support Manage MCP servers for your AI agents. Connect external services to extend your agent's capabilities. Attio Manage Attio CRM contacts, companies, and deals. HubSpot Sync contacts, companies, and marketing lifecycle for richer agent context. Stripe Look up customers, subscriptions, and invoices from billing. Salesforce Query accounts, opportunities, and CRM records your agents need in context. Sentry Pull issues, releases, and stack traces so agents debug against real errors. Try it PostHog Code is launching in Spring 2026. Join the waitlist to be the first to try it. Join the waitlist Get updates FAQ What is PostHog Code? What's the difference between PostHog AI and PostHog Code? Why is PostHog building a coding agent? Does it replace Cursor or Claude Code? What models and editors does it work with? What if I don't use PostHog yet? How does it decide what to work on? Where do tasks run – locally or in the cloud? Is my code sent to PostHog? Is my PostHog data safe? Can it modify my PostHog configuration? How much does it cost? Is it open source?

items.article_text: Grok API Company Colossus Careers News Shop SpaceX 𝕏 Try Grok May 06, 2026 New Compute Partnership with Anthropic SpaceXAI has signed an agreement with Anthropic to provide access to Colossus 1. SpaceXAI has signed an agreement with Anthropic to provide access to Colossus 1, one of the world’s largest and fastest-deployed AI supercomputers. Built from the ground up in record time, Colossus delivers unprecedented scale for AI training, fine-tuning, inference, and high-performance computing workloads. Colossus 1 features over 220,000 NVIDIA GPUs, including dense deployments of H100, H200, and next-generation GB200 accelerators. The cluster delivers extreme parallel performance for large language models, multimodal systems, scientific simulations, and generative AI at frontier scale. Anthropic plans to use this additional compute to directly improve capacity for Claude Pro and Claude Max subscribers. As part of this agreement, Anthropic also expressed interest in partnering to develop multiple gigawatts of orbital AI compute capacity. The compute required to train and operate the next generation of these systems is outpacing what terrestrial power, land, and cooling can deliver on the timelines that matter. SpaceX is the only organization with the launch cadence, mass-to-orbit economics, and constellation operations experience to make orbital compute a near-term engineering program rather than a research concept. If engineering challenges can be overcome, space-based compute offers near-limitless sustainable power with less impact on Earth. items.article_title_extracted: ‎Roll - Fresh film daily App - App Store
items.description:
What if creativity came from less, not more?

With Roll, you get a single film roll every day. You unbox it, and that’s your look for the day. No switching, no tweaking, no second-guessing. Just one perspective to explore.

Each roll is the same for everyone. All over the world, people are capturing their day through the exact same filter. The result is a shared visual language, where the difference isn’t the tool - it’s how you see.

There are no endless options here. No editing tools to hide behind. Just a simple constraint that pushes you to think differently and notice more.

The ordinary starts to feel new again. Light, color, and moments you might have ignored suddenly stand out, simply because you’re looking at them in a new way.

Roll is about slowing down, letting go of perfection, and rediscovering the joy of taking photos.

◼ Break out of filter fatigue
◼ Build a daily creative habit
◼ See how others interpret the same roll
◼ Focus on what matters, not what’s possible

Unbox your roll. Shoot your day. See what happens.
items.description_zh:
如果创造力源于“更少”，而非“更多”呢？

使用 Roll，你每天只会收到一卷胶卷。拆开它，这就是你今天唯一的视角。无需切换、无需调整、无需犹豫。只有一种视角，供你探索。

每个人收到的胶卷都一样。全世界的人们都在用完全相同的滤镜记录他们的一天。由此诞生了一种共享的视觉语言——区别不在于工具，而在于你如何观察。

这里没有无穷无尽的选项。没有编辑工具可以躲藏。只有一个简单的约束，迫使你换种方式思考，留意更多。

平凡的事物开始焕然一新。光线、色彩、那些你或许曾忽略的瞬间，突然变得醒目——只因你换了一种方式去看它们。

Roll 的意义在于慢下来，放下对完美的执念，重新发现拍照的乐趣。

◼ 摆脱滤镜疲劳
◼ 培养每日创意习惯
◼ 看看他人如何诠释同一卷胶卷
◼ 专注于重要的事，而非可能的事

拆开你的胶卷。拍摄你的一天。看看会发生什么。
items.title: ‎Roll - Fresh film daily App - App Store
items.title_zh: ‎Roll - 每日新鲜影片 App - App Store

items.article_title_extracted: K3s
items.description: K3s is a lightweight, certified Kubernetes distribution designed for IoT and Edge computing, ideal for production workloads in resource-constrained environments. It is easy to install with a compact binary size of under 70MB and supports both ARM64 and ARMv7 architectures, making it suitable for small devices like Raspberry Pi as well as larger servers. K3s simplifies the installation process and enhances security by reducing dependencies. Users can quickly get started by downloading the latest release and following simple commands to set up server and agent nodes. K3s is supported by Rancher and is recognized as a CNCF Sandbox Project.
items.description_zh: K3s 是一款轻量级、经过认证的 Kubernetes 发行版，专为物联网和边缘计算设计，非常适合资源受限环境中的生产工作负载。其安装简便，二进制文件体积小巧（不足70MB），并支持ARM64和ARMv7架构，因此既适用于树莓派等小型设备，也能在大型服务器上运行。K3s通过减少依赖项简化了安装流程并增强了安全性。用户只需下载最新版本，按照简单命令即可快速配置服务器和代理节点。K3s由Rancher提供支持，并被认定为CNCF沙箱项目。

items.article_text:
How we built a real-time video agent that transforms any single image — photorealistic human, cartoon mascot or fantasy creature — into a fully expressive conversational character at 24fps, with fast end-to-end latency. Zero fine-tuning required.
Runway Characters is a real-time video agent that turns a single reference image into a fully expressive, conversational video character. Built on GWM-1, our General World Model, it produces natural lip-sync, facial expressions and head motions at 24 frames per second (fps) in HD.
Characters has an effective 37 milliseconds of model time per frame (at more than 24 fps), and 1.75 seconds server-side turn-around from when the user stops speaking to when the character starts responding.
This post covers what makes Characters different: the breadth of what it can animate, how we made it real-time and the product ecosystem we built around the model.
The input to Characters is a single reference image. That's it. The system extracts style directly from the image, and immediately begins generating conversational video conditioned on real-time audio.
Below are 720p clips showing Characters animating a single reference image across styles—from photorealistic humans to cartoons, creatures and brand mascots—with natural, expressive conversation.
24fps in real time: Characters runs at an effective 37 milliseconds (ms) of model time per frame and 1.75 seconds server-side turn-around from end-of-speech to first response frame.
Generating HD video at 24fps in an interactive conversational loop is a fundamentally different engineering problem than offline video generation. Offline, you can spend seconds per frame. In a conversation, you have a frame budget of 42 ms (1/24 seconds) – and that's before audio processing, network transit and the perception of “liveness” that collapses if latency creeps up. In our live sessions, we achieve an effective 37 ms of model time per frame, and 1.75 seconds of server-side turn-around (voice agent + video pipeline) from when the user stops speaking to when the character starts responding.
We make this possible with GWM-1's autoregressive frame-by-frame generation: frames are produced sequentially and streamed to the client as they're generated, instead of iteratively denoising an entire clip.
We hit real-time 24fps because of overlap: the diffusion transformer and VAE decoder run concurrently via a pipeline split. That makes the effective iteration cost closer to max(diffusion_time, decode_time) than diffusion_time + decode_time.
Each iteration produces 4 pixel frames, so at 24fps we need one iteration every 167 ms (4/24 seconds). In our measurements, diffusion is 151 ms and VAE decoder is 119 ms; because decode is overlapped, it largely disappears from the critical path. That works out to an effective 37 ms of model time per frame (151 ms / 4 frames), under the 41 ms/frame real-time budget.
Without overlap, diffusion would consume most of the 167 ms budget and decode would push it over. With overlap, decode of frame N−1 runs at the same time as diffusion of frame N.
“Real-time” is also what the user feels end-to-end: the time from when they stop speaking to when the character starts responding. Putting it together: 1185 ms (voice agent) + 567 ms (video pipeline) = 1.75 seconds server-side latency in this session. Client↔server network adds 200 ms on each side (400 ms round trip), depending on connection quality.
The biggest lever to bring this down further is the voice agent: the video pipeline is already fast, and we expect the overall turn-around to drop as we improve voice agent response time.
Below are four of the key model- and systems-level optimizations that make this real-time performance possible:
We shard inference across devices, where the main constraint is communication overhead. We pipeline tensor‑parallel diffusion with VAE decoder on a dedicated device so decode doesn’t contend with the transformer.
Autoregressive video grows the KV cache every fra

items.article_text: Skip to main content Product Why Fathom Pricing Resources The Google Analytics Alternative Why you should ditch Google Analytics No cookies & GDPR compliant Actionable insights while staying compliant with privacy laws About us 100% independently owned, zero VC pressure Help Center & Docs API Blog Changelog Status Free trial Login Pricing Free trial Log in Menu Fathom acquires Gauges news Jack Ellis · Apr 20, 2026 We are so excited to announce that Fathom has acquired Gauges , the beautiful, real-time, simple analytics product that paved the way for companies like ours. A brief history Gauges was founded in 2011 by Ordered List, who looked at the website analytics landscape and thought, “Why can’t analytics be simple?” Their small but mighty team shipped an incredible product, which took off immediately. Nine months later, Ordered List (and Gauges) was acquired by GitHub. In 2017, GitHub sold Gauges to ReportGarden Inc, who had big plans for Gauges and kept it running for the next nine years. Then, in March 2026, ReportGarden made the hard decision to announce the sunset of Gauges. It was no longer feasible for them to maintain it whilst also actively building their main product. We immediately had Gauges customers emailing us about importing their data into Fathom, and I knew we had to do something, so I emailed their CEO. And then, on 15th April, we acquired Gauges. What happens now Fathom offers everything Gauges did and more, and we will be migrating all of their customers over to our platform whilst preserving their historical data. We’ll also soon be adding Air Traffic Live, popular with Gauges customers, to Fathom as a first-class feature. Thank you To the Ordered List team, thank you for building Gauges and for the beautifully simple codebase and data structure. To ReportGarden Inc, thank you for the smooth process of getting to a deal that worked for both of us. ReportGarden is now doubling down on their reporting hub and insights hub. You can learn more about them here . Return to the Fathom Analytics blog BIO Jack Ellis, CTO Recent blog posts Fathom acquires Gauges Announcing: Fathom V4 Fathom Analytics has been acquired I made a mistake Reducing our AWS bill by $100,000 Tired of how time consuming and complex Google Analytics can be? Try Fathom Analytics: Start a free trial Sign up for our monthly newsletter via email, or grab the RSS feed . Subscribe We've counted billions of pageviews for thousands of customers, all without ever compromising anyone's digital privacy. We hope you'll join us . 2018-2026 Fathom Analytics. Legal compliance Contact us Log in or Start a free trial

items.article_title_extracted: ‎Things take time — Logbook App - App Store
items.description:
Good things take time. Real progress is built through small things done consistently — it won’t feel like much, until it does.

Things take time is an app for tracking what matters, the small things, and seeing how they compound over time.


KEY FEATURES

• Track anything
From habits and routines to personal metrics, notes, and daily actions — build trackers that fit your life, not the other way around.

• See everything side by side
Most apps show one tracker at a time, which hides the bigger picture. This one shows them all together so you can see patterns across your month.

• Your month at a glance
See how small things add up across your month, so you can stay consistent and notice real progress.

• Built for ease of use
Tracking should feel effortless. Everything is designed to be fast, simple, and intuitive.

• Privacy first
Your data stays on your device or in your iCloud. Nothing is collected, and nothing is tracked.


Progress is often invisible in the moment. Things take time was built to make it visible — by showing how small things add up over time.


Terms & Privacy: https://github.com/chesterhow/things-take-time/blob/main/terms-privacy.md
items.description_zh:
好事多磨。真正的进步源于日复一日坚持的小事——起初或许微不足道，终将厚积薄发。

《好事多磨》是一款追踪重要事物、记录点滴小事，见证它们随时间累积效应的应用。

**核心功能**

• 随心追踪
从习惯养成、日常规律到个人数据、笔记和每日行动——打造契合你生活的追踪体系，而非让生活迁就工具。

• 全局纵览
多数应用一次只展示单一追踪项，反而掩盖了全局图景。本应用将所有数据并列呈现，让你轻松发现月度规律。

• 月度概览
直观看到微小积累如何汇聚成月度成果，助你保持连贯性，察觉真实进步。

• 便捷易用
追踪本该毫不费力。所有设计都追求快速、简洁、直观。

• 隐私至上
你的数据仅存于设备或iCloud中。绝不收集任何信息，不做任何追踪。

进步往往在当下难以察觉。《好事多磨》正是为了让进步可视化而生——通过展现微小积累如何随时间产生复利效应。

条款与隐私：https://github.com/chesterhow/things-take-time/blob/main/terms-privacy.md

items.article_text: THREE.JS ROADMAP Assets Courses Blog FAQ Login Sign Up Asset Three.js Sky Pro A complete, real-time sky system for Three.js. Volumetric clouds, dynamic atmosphere, and physically-inspired lighting. Coming Soon — Join the waitlist Join Waitlist On this page Features Features Three.js Sky Pro is a production-ready sky rendering engine built on Three.js WebGPU. It gives you a complete, dynamic sky out of the box — volumetric clouds, atmospheric scattering, sun lighting, and tone mapping — all rendered in real time with no baked textures or static skyboxes. Use the included demo to tune the sky to your scene. Adjust cloud coverage, density, and shape until it looks right, then export your settings directly into your project. Drag the sun across the sky and the whole atmosphere shifts — clouds pick up warm light at the edges, glow from within near sunset, and cast nsoft shadows through the layer. Add wind and everything drifts naturally. Designed to run on all devices, from mobile to high-refresh desktop displays. What You Get Complete drop-in sky system for Three.js projects Volumetric 3D clouds with full creative control over shape, density, and detail Dynamic atmosphere with configurable horizon, zenith, and sun colors Physically-inspired cloud lighting with scattering, extinction, and inner glow Wind-driven animation and real-time time-of-day changes Tunable quality settings for any performance budget WebGPU-powered with Three.js Shading Language (TSL) Links Home Assets Courses FAQ Legal Privacy Policy Cookie Policy Terms of Service Contact support@threejsroadmap.com X LinkedIn Instagram YouTube GitHub © 2026 Three.js Roadmap. All rights reserved. Privacy Settings

items.article_title_extracted: Copy Fail — CVE-2026-31431
items.description: Copy Fail (CVE-2026-31431): a 732-byte Linux LPE — straight-line, no race, no per-distro offsets. Same Python script roots Ubuntu, Amazon Linux, RHEL, SUSE since 2017. Page-cache write bypasses on-disk file-integrity tools and crosses container boundaries. Found by Xint Code.
items.description_zh: Copy Fail (CVE-2026-31431)：一个 732 字节的 Linux 本地提权漏洞——直线攻击，无需竞争条件，无需针对不同发行版的偏移量。同一 Python 脚本即可在 Ubuntu、Amazon Linux、RHEL、SUSE（自 2017 年起）上获取 root 权限。通过绕过页面缓存写入，规避磁盘文件完整性检查工具，并跨越容器边界。由 Xint Code 发现。
items.title: Copy Fail — CVE-2026-31431

items.article_text:
Response Caching: Zero Cost for Identical Requests
Brian Thomas ·
You can now add X-OpenRouter-Cache: true
to your chat completions, responses, messages, or embeddings requests to start caching identical calls. The first call hits the provider and gets billed normally. Every identical call after that returns the same response in a tiny fraction of the time, with zero tokens billed.
[View the response caching docs(opens in new tab)](https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/features/response-caching)
What it does
Response caching sits in front of the model provider. When you send a request with caching enabled, OpenRouter hashes the request body, model, API key, and streaming mode into a cache key. If an identical request was made before and hasn't expired, the cached response comes back immediately. No provider call, no token consumption, no charge.
Both streaming and non-streaming requests work. Cached streaming responses replay through the same pipeline, so your client code doesn't need to change. Text, images, audio, documents, and tool calls all cache normally. Multimodal inputs (base64 images, audio clips, file attachments) are included in the cache key hash. One caveat: very large multimodal payloads that get offloaded internally for processing aren't eligible for caching. Standard-sized requests cache fine.
Response caching is separate from prompt caching. Prompt caching (which many providers offer natively) reduces the cost of the prompt portion when messages share a common prefix. Response caching skips the provider entirely and returns the full response from OpenRouter's edge cache.
Reduces response times from seconds to milliseconds
Cached responses come back in 80-300ms, most of which is serialization and network. The cache lookup itself averages 4ms. For comparison, a typical uncached request to Gemini 2.5 Flash takes about 1.3 seconds, Kimi K2.6 takes 4.6 seconds, and GPT-5.5 takes 9.1 seconds. Cache hits are billed at zero: no prompt tokens, no completion tokens, no charge.
Enable it with a request header or with presets
Add the X-OpenRouter-Cache: true
header to each API call you want to be eligible:
Presets. Enable caching for all requests using a specific preset by setting cache_enabled: true
[in the preset config(opens in new tab)](https://openrouter.ai/workspaces/default/presets). No header needed on individual requests.
You can control how long responses stay cached with X-OpenRouter-Cache-TTL
(1 second to 24 hours, default 5 minutes). Need a fresh response? Send X-OpenRouter-Cache-Clear: true
to bust the cache for that specific request.
Response headers tell you what happened: X-OpenRouter-Cache-Status: HIT
or MISS
, plus X-OpenRouter-Cache-Age
and X-OpenRouter-Cache-TTL
so you can see exactly how the cache is performing.
Where it helps most
Agent retries. When an agent workflow fails partway through, you can retry from the top. Cached steps return instantly and for free, so you only pay for the new work.
Test suites. Run your LLM-backed tests repeatedly without burning tokens. After the first run populates the cache, subsequent runs are deterministic and free.
Repeated context processing. If your app sends the same prompt to the same model (same system prompt, same user input, same parameters), only the first call costs anything.
Available now across most generation endpoints
The cache is scoped to your API key. Different keys (even under the same account) don't share cache entries.
The feature works across /chat/completions
, /responses
, /messages
, and /embeddings
. Other endpoints — legacy /completions
, /audio/speech
(TTS), /audio/transcriptions
(STT), /rerank
, and video generation — are not yet supported. It's currently in beta, and we're watching how it performs before locking down the API surface.
Cache hits don't count toward provider rate limits (since the request never reaches the provider), and they're visible in your [Activity log(opens in new tab)](https://openrouter.ai/activity) with a cache indi

[![MatX](images/logo-site.svg)![](images/logo-site-hover.svg)](/) 

[Company](https://matx.com/) [Research](/research) [Jobs](https://matx.com/jobs) 

[Company](https://matx.com/) [Research](/research) [Jobs](https://matx.com/jobs) 

# High-throughput chips for LLMs

Our goal is to make the best chips physically possible for the large model needs of frontier labs.

The [MatX One](https://matx.com/research/series%5Fb) chip delivers higher throughput than any announced product while also matching the best latencies of any products. For training and prefill, it excels on FLOPS; for decode and RL it excels on latency, FLOPS, and long-context support.

What we offer

* The highest FLOPS/mm2.
* Weights are typically in SRAM, for low latency. Allows >2000 output tokens/second for large 100-layer MoE models.
* KVs are typically in HBM, to support long context well.
* The most scale-up interconnect of any product.
* Excellent scale-out interconnect, supporting clusters with hundreds of thousands of chips.
* A programming model that gives you direct control over the hardware.

Target workloads

* Training, RL, inference prefill, inference decode.
* Large MoE models work well, as do large dense models. There is no upper limit on model size.
* No small models, no convolutions, no recommenders.

Investors

Jane Street, Situational Awareness LP, Spark Capital, Nat Friedman and Daniel Gross's fund, Triatomic Capital, and [many others](https://matx.com/research/series%5Fb).

© MatX 

[Contact](mailto:founders@matx.com) [ ](https://www.linkedin.com/company/matxcomputing/) [ ](https://x.com/matxcomputing)

items.article_title_extracted: 🦀 Crabbox Docs - Crabbox Docs
items.description:
Crabbox is a shared remote testbox system designed for OpenClaw maintainers, allowing developers to keep their local workflows while offloading compute tasks to cloud resources. Key features include:

1. **Local Loop, Remote Box**: Developers sync their local files to a rented Linux machine for remote execution.
2. **Credential Management**: A Cloudflare Worker manages provider credentials, while the local CLI only needs a bearer token.
3. **Cost Management**: Operates with TTL-limited machines, monthly spending caps, and usage tracking on a per-user basis.
4. **Warm Boxes**: `crabbox warmup` allows users to maintain a ready state for subsequent runs, improving efficiency.
5. **Server Flexibility**: Can utilize Hetzner or AWS Spot instances when server capacity is constrained.
6. **Integration with Actions**: Reuses GitHub Actions in local runs.

### How It Works:
- The CLI connects to a Cloudflare Worker to provision a cloud instance, sync files, execute commands, and manage resource releases.

### Installation & Quick Start:
- Install via Homebrew, log in to configure, and execute commands using `crabbox run` and `crabbox warmup`.

### Additional Tools:
- Includes an OpenClaw plugin for integrating Crabbox operations.

### Documentation:
- Various resources available to help understand how to use and operate Crabbox.
items.description_zh:
Crabbox 是一个专为 OpenClaw 维护者设计的共享远程测试盒系统，允许开发者保持本地工作流程的同时，将计算任务卸载到云端资源。主要特性包括：

1. **本地循环，远程执行**：开发者将本地文件同步到租赁的 Linux 机器上进行远程执行。
2. **凭证管理**：Cloudflare Worker 管理提供商凭证，本地 CLI 仅需持有 bearer token。
3. **成本管理**：通过 TTL 限制的机器、月度消费上限和按用户的使用追踪进行运营。
4. **预热盒**：`crabbox warmup` 允许用户维护就绪状态，提升后续运行的效率。
5. **服务器灵活性**：当服务器容量受限时，可使用 Hetzner 或 AWS Spot 实例。
6. **与 Actions 集成**：在本地运行中复用 GitHub Actions。

### 工作原理：
- CLI 连接 Cloudflare Worker 以配置云实例、同步文件、执行命令并管理资源释放。

### 安装与快速开始：
- 通过 Homebrew 安装，登录配置后使用 `crabbox run` 和 `crabbox warmup` 执行命令。

### 附加工具：
- 包含用于集成 Crabbox 操作的 OpenClaw 插件。

### 文档：
- 提供多种资源帮助理解如何使用和操作 Crabbox。
items.title: 🦀 Crabbox Docs - Crabbox Docs
items.title_zh: 🦀 Crabbox 文档 - Crabbox 文档

items.article_text: Products Community Learn Pricing Downloads Rive Blog Free Rive AI Agent Starting today, the Rive Agent is available to everyone, including Free users. AI should make it easier for anyone to learn and build in Rive, regardless of plan. by Guido Rosso - Thursday, April 30, 2026 What just landed The #1 thing we heard: the Agent eats credits too fast. So we introduced more efficient models that cost significantly less, and added an Ask mode for questions that don't need the full Agent. This is very new and we're releasing it early to get your feedback. Here's how AI works across plans: Free — Agent for everyone. It has a capacity, you use it with each prompt, and it recharges hourly. Cadet — Same free Agent modes. Cadet's purpose is to be the most affordable way to export Rive files, not a premium AI plan. At $9/mo, we can't offer a compelling premium AI experience (most paid AI plans start at $20/mo for a reason). We'd rather give you free modes that work well than premium access that runs out fast. New Cadet plans don't include AI credits. If you were already on Cadet before this change, you keep your $5/mo and access to the premium plans. That's grandfathered. Voyager — Premium models, $20/mo in recurring credits that refresh monthly, plus the ability to top up. What's still coming We're working on a bunch of optimizations based on your feedback, which will be landing soon. The Agent will be smarter about context, sending only what it needs, and picking tools more selectively. We're also exploring different ways to let you connect your external agents to the Rive Editor (we've heard your requests to keep working on MCP and the ability to bring your own API keys). This is early We're watching closely and shipping fast. Come tell us what's working (and what isn't) in the Rive Community or Discord . Start building production-ready graphics Get Started Join our newsletter Get all the latest Rive news delivered to your inbox. U s e t h e m a s a g u i d e , n o t a f i n a l a n s w e r . S c o r e s r e f l e c t o u r o w n m e t h o d o l o g y a n d m a y d i f f e r f r o m o t h e r A E O r e p o r t s . AI doesn’t rank pages. It picks answers. AI doesn’t rank pages. It picks answers. AI doesn’t rank pages. It picks answers. Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) is the practice of making your content easy for AI systems like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Gemini to find, understand, and quote. As more people get answers directly from AI instead of clicking search results, being invisible to AI means being invisible to your audience. AI answers are replacing search clicks Over 60% of searches now end without a click. If AI doesn’t surface your site, users may never find you at all. Structure and trust signals matter more than ever AI models favor pages with clear metadata, structured content, and authoritative sources. Bad structure means your content gets skipped entirely. Most websites aren’t ready — yours can be The vast majority of sites still lack the basic signals AI needs. A few targeted fixes can move you from invisible to frequently cited. items.article_text:
Astro 6.2 is here! This release brings an experimental custom logger with built-in JSON output, perfect for structured logging and coding agents. It also includes a new SVG optimizer API, a font file URL helper, and more.
Explore what’s new in this release:
[SVG optimizer](https://astro.build#svg-optimizer)[Experimental Logger](https://astro.build#experimental-logger)[Experimental:](https://astro.build#experimental-getfontfileurl)getFontFileURL()
[Astro v7 alpha](https://astro.build#astro-v7-alpha)allowedHosts
for preview servers"jsx"
option forcompressHTML
To upgrade an existing project, use the automated @astrojs/upgrade
CLI tool. Alternatively, upgrade manually by running the upgrade command for your package manager:
SVG optimizer
Astro’s experimental SVG optimization has been redesigned with a new svgOptimizer
option that replaces the previous svgo
flag. Instead of being tied directly to SVGO, the new API introduces an SvgOptimizer
interface that any optimization library can implement. Astro ships with a built-in svgoOptimizer()
based on [SVGO](https://svgo.dev/), but you can now swap in alternatives like [OxVG](https://github.com/noahbald/oxvg).
When enabled, all imported SVG files used as components are optimized at build time for smaller file sizes and better performance. You can pass a [SVGO configuration object](https://svgo.dev/) to customize the optimization:
If you were previously using experimental.svgo
, update your config to use the new svgOptimizer
option instead.
For more information, see the [experimental SVG optimization docs](https://docs.astro.build/en/reference/experimental-flags/svg-optimization/).
Experimental Logger
It’s now possible to control the Astro logger with custom loggers. As part of the experimental feature, Astro ships with built-in loggers, such as a JSON logger. If you use coding agents to work with Astro, structured logs will definitely help your workflow.
JSON logger
You can enable JSON logging via configuration using experimental.logger
, or via CLI using the flag --experimentalJson
:
Custom logger
You can also create your own custom logger by implementing the correct interface:
For more information on enabling and using this feature in your project, see the [Experimental Logger docs](https://docs.astro.build/en/reference/experimental-flags/logger/).
For a complete overview and to give feedback on this experimental API, see the [Custom logger RFC](https://github.com/withastro/roadmap/blob/logger/proposals/0059-custom-logger.md).
Experimental: getFontFileURL()
You can now load font file data during prerendering using the new experimental_getFontFileURL()
helper exported from astro:assets
. This makes it possible to use Astro-managed fonts with tools like [Satori](https://github.com/vercel/satori) for generating Open Graph images at build time, something that previously required workarounds with undocumented internal paths.
For more information, see the [Fonts API documentation](https://docs.astro.build/en/guides/fonts/#accessing-font-data-programmatically).
Astro v7 alpha
Work on the next major version of Astro has begun. The first Astro 7 alpha includes two big changes:
Vite 8 support
Astro 7 upgrades to [Vite 8](https://vite.dev/blog/announcing-vite8), the latest major version of the build tool that powers Astro. This is a breaking change for Astro integrations and plugins that depend on Vite internals, but most Astro users should be able to upgrade without any changes to their project code.
Stable Rust compiler
The Rust-based Astro compiler, previously available as an experimental flag (experimental.rustCompiler
), is now the default and only compiler in Astro 7. The Rust compiler delivers significantly faster build times compared to the previous Go-based compiler. The experimental.rustCompiler
flag is no longer needed and can be removed from your config.
Try it out
The Astro 7 alpha is available for early testing:
We’d love to hear your feedback! Please report any issues you encounte

items.article_text: iPhone 专区 iPhone iPad Mac Vision Watch 搜索 Today 游戏 App 平台 iPhone iPad Mac Vision Watch MyDooors-自然疗愈 · 放松解压 · 收集与装扮 新疆｜手绘场景｜每日礼物｜房间装扮｜安静片刻 仅适用于 iPhone 免费 · App 内购买 · 专为 iPhone 设计。未针对 macOS 验证。 分享 17个评分 5.0 年龄 4+ 岁 类别 健康健美 开发者 Hangzhou Rossy Rossy Technology Co., Ltd 语言 ZH + 1 种语言 大小 178.2 MB iPhone ▶工作累了，生活节奏快，想有个透透气的空间？ 打开My Dooors，打开一扇门，进入自然，聆听大自然的声音，你可以做任何你想做的事情，或者什么都不做。就像一场短暂的旅行，带回一个小礼物，把自然带回家，自由地装扮自己的空间，在自己的专属空间里，慢慢地生活。 ▶在这里你可以： （1）进入手绘的新疆风景里，沉浸在自然白噪声中，体验手绘的治愈感，找回身体的放松节奏 雾气逐渐散去的禾木、冬日初雪的牧场、喀纳斯河边的草地、车上沿途看风景……风声、鸟鸣、牛哞、远处村庄里炊火声、脚踩在雪地的声音……丰富立体的自然声音，把你瞬间拉进其中，跳脱当下。每一种声音都经过手绘场景的重新诠释，让你在喧嚣世界里找到自己的安静角落。 （2）每天获得一件自然礼物和它的故事，共100件礼物 玩偶、照片、地毯、植物等，禾木百年老树的照片，比脸还大的烤馕照片，赛里木湖的白番红花，哈萨克毡房小摆件，森林雪景地毯等……每个礼物都有它的故事，娓娓道来 （3）用礼物装扮你的专属空间 你可以把礼物自由地摆进自己的房间，做出只属于你的 cozy 小世界。也可以在房间里专注、休息、阅读，听听音乐，看着窗外发发呆也好。 （4）每天只需要一点点时间，让生活慢下来 在这里，你没有任务、没有压力。 你可以放松、专注、发呆…… 也可以什么都不做。 ▶为什么会有 MyDooors？ 灵感来自我们的新疆旅行： “那天，我们在山里等待雾散开，突然之间，世界安静、辽阔、温柔，我希望把那种感觉放进一扇门里，让更多人能随时回到那里。” ▶付费与订阅说明 MyDooors 提供以下付费方式： * 月度旅程（订阅）：按月使用 * 年度旅程（订阅）：按年使用 * 永久通行证（一次性购买）：一次开通，永久使用 部分功能需要订阅 MyDooors Pro 才可使用，包括完整的自然场景与小屋体验。 ▶订阅自动续费规则： * 购买后，费用将从您的 Apple ID 账户中扣除 * 订阅将在当前周期结束前 24 小时内自动续费，并从账户扣除续费费用 * 如需取消订阅，请在当前周期结束前至少 24 小时前，在 App Store 账户设置中关闭自动续费 * 取消后，当前周期结束前仍可继续使用已订阅内容 * 若提供免费试用，试用期结束后将自动转为付费订阅并从账户扣费（如未在试用期结束前取消） ▶解锁内容 开通 MyDooors Pro 后，您将获得完整体验： * 解锁全部自然场景（山间、森林、湖谷等） * 解锁完整礼物与故事内容（持续收集与更新） * 解锁小屋布置功能，自由构建属于自己的空间 ▶隐私政策： https://denim-trollius-d2e.notion.site/Privacy-Policy-2e08c5dc9fa880ea9516e77ef7bfd757 ▶用户协议： https://denim-trollius-d2e.notion.site/Terms-of-Use-2e08c5dc9fa880ee8bb5f1ac9a348865 ————MyDooors 的创作者———— 我们是栗枣和 Andy， 设计师、插画师，也是开发者。 我们用画画的方式， 做一些治愈、放松的产品。 MyDooors 里的所有元素， 都来自一笔一画的手绘。 ▶关注我们 邮箱：shaluodebai@gmail.com 小红书：@肉喜子工作坊 Instagram：@wadebryant23 Threads：@wadebryant23 X：@wadebryant23 更多 评分及评论 5.0 满分 5 分 17 个评分 设计很棒的产品，呆的每一刻都感到治愈 5月6日 原来是yang呀 关注好久的 app 终于发布了，好开心。点开「逃离远方」，日常信息过载太需要这种远方了。小红花一样的小孩朝门走去，手机每一步都有震动，像自己真实踩在路上。进入「远观禾木」，雨声淅沥，远眺村庄有种一览众山小的满足。最惊喜「黄色的树」，稻草人挥动双手像在指挥，接着歌曲响起，互动超有趣。翻完九个场景，像回看人生走过的路。最后回到小家，与自己和解。一扇门，从紧绷到自然远方，这个巧思真的很棒。 开发者回复 items.article_title_extracted: ‎MyDooors-自然疗愈 · 放松解压 · 收集与装扮 App - App Store
items.description:
▶工作累了，生活节奏快，想有个透透气的空间？
打开My Dooors，打开一扇门，进入自然，聆听大自然的声音，你可以做任何你想做的事情，或者什么都不做。就像一场短暂的旅行，带回一个小礼物，把自然带回家，自由地装扮自己的空间，在自己的专属空间里，慢慢地生活。

▶在这里你可以：
（1）进入手绘的新疆风景里，沉浸在自然白噪声中，体验手绘的治愈感，找回身体的放松节奏
雾气逐渐散去的禾木、冬日初雪的牧场、喀纳斯河边的草地、车上沿途看风景……风声、鸟鸣、牛哞、远处村庄里炊火声、脚踩在雪地的声音……丰富立体的自然声音，把你瞬间拉进其中，跳脱当下。每一种声音都经过手绘场景的重新诠释，让你在喧嚣世界里找到自己的安静角落。

（2）每天获得一件自然礼物和它的故事，共100件礼物
玩偶、照片、地毯、植物等，禾木百年老树的照片，比脸还大的烤馕照片，赛里木湖的白番红花，哈萨克毡房小摆件，森林雪景地毯等……每个礼物都有它的故事，娓娓道来

（3）用礼物装扮你的专属空间
你可以把礼物自由地摆进自己的房间，做出只属于你的 cozy 小世界。也可以在房间里专注、休息、阅读，听听音乐，看着窗外发发呆也好。

（4）每天只需要一点点时间，让生活慢下来
在这里，你没有任务、没有压力。 你可以放松、专注、发呆…… 也可以什么都不做。

▶为什么会有 MyDooors？
灵感来自我们的新疆旅行： “那天，我们在山里等待雾散开，突然之间，世界安静、辽阔、温柔，我希望把那种感觉放进一扇门里，让更多人能随时回到那里。”

▶付费与订阅说明
MyDooors 提供以下付费方式：
* 月度旅程（订阅）：按月使用
* 年度旅程（订阅）：按年使用
* 永久通行证（一次性购买）：一次开通，永久使用
部分功能需要订阅 MyDooors Pro 才可使用，包括完整的自然场景与小屋体验。

▶订阅自动续费规则：
* 购买后，费用将从您的 Apple ID 账户中扣除

items.article_title_extracted: Casa – your home, on autopilot
items.description:
Casa offers a comprehensive membership service designed to simplify home management with a focus on convenience and expert assistance. Key features include:

1. **Home Understanding**: Casa learns about every aspect of your home, documenting items, data points, and serial numbers.
2. **Expert Services**: Members have access to experienced handymen and a dedicated concierge to handle home projects and tasks efficiently, including maintenance and grocery pickups.
3. **Daily Chores Assistance**: The service covers a wide range of tasks from minor fixes (like tightening towel racks) to scheduling pest control and lawn care.
4. **Membership Benefits**: Each month, members receive 1.5 hours of handyman service, with rollover hours, along with unlimited concierge support.
5. **Automated Maintenance**: Users can subscribe to tailored care plans that automate scheduling and management to prevent costly repairs.
6. **Home Inventory**: Members benefit from a detailed inventory of their home’s assets, which aids in maintenance and upgrades.
7. **Pricing**: Membership costs $199/month, with no long-term commitments or hidden fees. 

The service is available in the San Francisco Bay Area and Los Angeles.
items.description_zh:
Casa 提供全面的会员服务，旨在通过便捷与专家协助简化家庭管理。核心功能包括：

1. **房屋认知**：Casa 会全面了解您的住宅，记录物品、数据点和序列号。
2. **专家服务**：会员可联系经验丰富的杂工和专属管家，高效处理家居项目与日常事务，包括维修保养和生鲜代购。
3. **日常杂务协助**：服务覆盖从拧紧毛巾架等小修小补，到安排虫害防治和草坪养护等各类任务。
4. **会员权益**：每月享有1.5小时杂工服务（可累积），并配备无限次管家支持。
5. **自动化维护**：用户可订阅定制化养护方案，自动安排管理流程，避免高额维修费用。
6. **房屋资产清单**：会员可获取详细的住宅资产清单，助力维护与升级规划。
7. **定价**：会员费为199美元/月，无长期合约或隐藏费用。

该服务目前覆盖旧金山湾区和洛杉矶地区。

items.article_text:
April 24, 2026
Mediabunny now supports HLS!
Vanilagy
Mediabunny v1.42.0 adds support for HTTP Live Streaming (HLS). This has been in the making for the last ~4 months and is, by far, the biggest addition to Mediabunny since its original release. Like the rest of Mediabunny, it has been implemented from scratch with zero dependencies and is tiny (adds about ~30 kB of additional bundle size).
If you wanna jump right into detailed guides for reading and writing HLS, check out [Reading HLS](https://mediabunny.dev/guide/reading-hls) and [Writing HLS](https://mediabunny.dev/guide/writing-hls). For the full release notes, see [v1.42.0](https://github.com/Vanilagy/mediabunny/releases/tag/v1.42.0).
What is HTTP Live Streaming (HLS)? [](https://mediabunny.dev#what-is-http-live-streaming-hls)
For those unfamiliar, HLS is a protocol used to stream media over the internet using HTTP. At its core, it chunks a long piece of media (like a TV episode) into many short segments, each of which is individually addressable via HTTP, and then provides an index called a "playlist" to the user telling them about the available segments. The client then requests whichever segments it needs.
HLS also enables multiple variants and renditions of the same contents. Typically, this means the same video/audio content encoded with multiple bitrates, resolutions and codecs, and multiple audio tracks, one for each language.
Supported features [](https://mediabunny.dev#supported-features)
One of the reasons that HLS has been in the works for so long is because I've been extremely thorough with the supported feature set.
Mediabunny supports:
- HLS reading and writing
- both VOD and live content
- both unencrypted and encrypted content (including DRM-protected content)
- an arbitrary number of video and audio tracks
- any configuration of variant streams and media renditions
- all segment formats (MPEG-TS, CMAF, fMP4, ADTS, MP3, WAV, ...)
- all codecs (H.264, HEVC, AV1, AAC, MP3, WAVE, AC-3, E-AC-3, ...)
- full lazy loading of track metadata and media, minimizing HTTP requests
- logarithmic seeking performance
- single-file segments via #EXT-X-BYTERANGE and HTTP range requests
- track metadata information (language, name, primary track, autoselect, ...)
- full master playlist configuration for writing
- datetime-stamped media data via #EXT-X-PROGRAM-DATE-TIME
- mid-stream discontinuities
- I-frame only video tracks via #EXT-X-I-FRAME-STREAM-INF
- more, probably
Most of the above features are implemented symmetrically, meaning they are available for both reading and writing operations.
To be fully transparent, these features are not yet supported:
- Subtitles (WebVTT, CEA-608/708, ...)
- ID3v2 metadata extraction
- Writing encrypted segments
- Low-latency HLS
- Built-in analytics, ABR, or CMCD (this is for the user to do)
Difference to existing solutions like hls.js [](https://mediabunny.dev#difference-to-existing-solutions-like-hls-js)
The way Mediabunny enables interaction with HLS playlists is fundamentally different from how existing tools such as [hls.js](https://github.com/video-dev/hls.js) (give them a star!) do it. To put it simply, hls.js is to Mediabunny what a <video>
element is to FFmpeg's C API: one offers a simple, playback-focused developer experience while the other provides fine-grained control over tracks, media samples, decoding, and much more.
Mediabunny is not an HLS player. Mediabunny can be used to build an HLS player, and it can do many things beyond that.
What can it do? [](https://mediabunny.dev#what-can-it-do)
The API surface added by the HLS update is vast and I obviously can't cover it in this announcement. But, here are just some cool things that Mediabunny now enables:
Downloading an HLS playlist as an MP4 [](https://mediabunny.dev#downloading-an-hls-playlist-as-an-mp4)
By using the Conversion API, you can just do this:
import { ... } from 'mediabunny';
const input = new Input({
source: new UrlSource('https://example.com/playlist.m3u8'

items.article_text:
[ Read Now](https://about.divine.video/blog/divine-launches-in-app-stores/)
500,000 classic Vines with new content from OG Viners including Lele Pons, Jimmy Here, MightyDuck and Jack & Jack
“(With Divine)… they are finally correcting every mistake,” Jack Dorsey
[Divine](http://divine.video/), the new six-second looping video app that reimagines the spirit of early internet human creativity by restoring Vine classics and banning AI slop, today launched in the Apple App Store and on Google Play.
Divine was announced in beta at the end of last year with a vision to put creative power back in human hands, and has since restored access to approximately 500,000 archived Vine videos from nearly 100k OG Vine creators including Lele Pons, Hannah Stocking, Jack and Jack, King Bach and Logan and Jake Paul, while enabling new six-second creations.
While rooted in the cultural legacy of short-form video, Divine is already seeing OG Viners including [Lele Pons](https://lelepons.divine.video/), [JimmyHere](https://jimmyhere.divine.video/), [MightyDuck](https://mightyduck.divine.video/), and [Jack and Jack,](https://jackandjackofficial.divine.video/) reclaim their accounts, curate their classics and create new content.
Lele Pons, whose career took-off on Vine while she was in high school, reached out to Divine’s founder, Rabble, within hours of hearing about the app back in November.
“Many of us came from Vine and it was the beginning of everything. An iconic app. It was such a key moment in my own personal journey, and in internet culture, it makes me so happy to see these early classics brought back to life, and to have the chance to make new ones,” said Pons
JimmyHere said: “Vine to me was the golden age of short form content. No AI. No crazy brand deals. Just a bunch of people with really crappy phone quality trying to entertain. With Divine we’re going back to basics. I always felt the six-second restriction wasn’t as much of a restriction, as an art form.”
MightyDuck said: “I would be nothing without Vine… I am so excited about how my videos are about to resurface on Divine… and so excited to make new six-second content… it feels like I’m going home to where I belong!”
Jack Dorsey has provided grant funding for Divine through [And Other Stuff,](http://andotherstuff.org/) the collective he funded to create and steward freedom tech through open tools, protocols, and applications that strengthen human liberty.
Accordingly, Divine has been built on the following principles:
- Creator ownership: creators retain control over their content and hence, their livelihoods
- Human-first content: a place for authentic, non-AI-generated media
- Deliberate design: focused on creativity and constraint over engagement for an ad algorithm
- Open and transparent: built on an open protocol, Nostr, with open source code
“By bringing back Vine on a decentralized network, they are finally correcting every mistake,” said Jack Dorsey.
“It is no secret that we didn’t find a business model for Vine. A founding principle for Divine is that creators will always be in full control of their content and followers, enabling them to create and grow their own revenue streams. I anticipate that Divine will provide a host of tools and services to support the growth of the creator economy,” said Dorsey.
As Divine emerges from beta, what started as an experiment to restore a formative era of internet culture is evolving into a place for creative expression that gives creators full agency over their content and their followers.
“Divine began as a personal project to reconnect with a time when the internet felt creative, open, and unquestionably human,” said Rabble, founder of Divine, and an original member of the team that created Twitter. “The overwhelming response we got to our initial announcement has turned my side project into more of a movement. The app launch is less about nostalgia, and more an antidote to what social media has become.
“Last year I crafted a [new soci

items.article_text: Skip to content Search for: Search Menu Bear.app Newsletter More Tips Updates News Interviews Support Search Settings Night Mode Text Size A Bear: Flexible notes, beautiful writing on Mac, iPad, iPhone, and Apple Watch Bear 2.8: BearCLI, Claude Connector, and MCP server Posted on Published April 29, 2026 April 29, 2026 by Zowie Huang AI has become a genuinely useful part of how a lot of people work, and we’ve spent a good while thinking about what that means for Bear. We didn’t want to bolt something on, hand your notes to a third party. Instead, we built something that gets out of the way and lets you decide what connects to Bear and how. Today we’re introducing BearCLI with Claude Connector and MCP server to open a whole new world for developers, power users, and anyone who wants to connect Bear to the tools they already use. Bear CLI BearCLI is a command-line tool bundled directly inside Bear. It lets you read, search, create, and edit notes from outside the app from a terminal, a script, or any tool that can run a shell command. Run bearcli -h to see everything it can do. For full installation instructions, see the support page . Claude Connector For Claude users, this is the most direct way to put your notes to work. Install it from Bear’s top menu Help → Advanced → Install Claude Connector , and Bear handles the rest. After that, you can open Claude and have a real conversation with your notes: ask questions, make changes, or create new ones, all in natural language. It’s built on BearCLI under the hood, so everything that works in the CLI works here too. Once you set up, the kinds of things you can ask become surprisingly useful, and occasionally a little philosophical: “Find all notes tagged #todo that haven’t been edited in over 30 days” “List all notes I created this week, sorted by last modified” “Find all empty notes and move them to the trash” “Based on everything in my notes, what kind of person do you think I am?” That last one is our favorite. MCP Server The MCP Server is for tools that speak the MCP protocol like Claude Code or certain IDEs. If you’re building workflows or working in an environment that supports MCP, bearcli mcp-server gives those tools direct access to your Bear notes with no extra setup. You can read here for more information about MCP server and how to use it. Your notes, your tools When we chose Markdown as Bear’s foundation, the reason wasn’t just technical. It was because an open format means your notes aren’t locked to any single app or use case. Your notes stay yours, whatever happens. We think about the CLI the same way. It’s a general-purpose tool that doesn’t know or care what’s on the other end. Nothing connects to your notes unless you set it up, and no notes are shared with any tool until you choose to. This just gives you more ways to use them. Try it now! Update Bear now on macOS to try it out. Kind reminder: It’s always a good idea to make a backup before you start anything adventurous. For the full setup guide and command reference, see the support page . Reach out to us on our Community Forum , Reddit , X (Twitter) , Bluesky , or directly at bear@shinyfrog.net to tell us what you build with BearCLI, we’d love to know! Happy writing! 🐻❤️📝 Mac updates Updates « Bear Beyond Text: Working with Images, PDFs, and more Bear at Apple Developer Academy Naples » Shiny Frog, ltd. Community Forum X(Twitter) Reddit RSS Loading Comments... Write a Comment... Email (Required) Name (Required) Website

items.article_title_extracted: Dynamic Carousel
items.description: The content discusses carousel controls, focusing on the duration (600ms) and various easing functions for slide motion, including easeInQuad, easeOutQuad, easeInOutQuad, and others. It explains that the easing affects only the motion of slides while video frames progress linearly. Additionally, the 'Drag Freeze' feature enables users to pause the video at 50% during dragging and resumes playback of the last 50% upon release.
items.description_zh: 内容讨论了轮播控制，重点介绍了滑动动画的持续时间（600毫秒）以及多种缓动函数，包括 easeInQuad、easeOutQuad、easeInOutQuad 等。文中说明缓动仅影响幻灯片的运动，而视频帧的播放仍保持线性推进。此外，“拖拽冻结”功能允许用户在拖拽时将视频暂停在50%处，并在释放后继续播放剩余50%的内容。
items.title: Dynamic Carousel

items.article_text:
Whatever AI platform you’re building new sites and apps with these days, getting a database thrown into the mix is a given. The agent will do the scaffolding and create the schema and code for you, which goes a pretty long way.
When we launched Netlify DB, the beta of Netlify Database, about a year ago (decades in AI-years), we had a more modest goal: we provisioned a database for you to use however you wanted, with or without AI. That was a nice shortcut to provisioning a database, but it quickly got behind today’s standards.
So when it came time to plan beyond the beta, the typical path would have been to tighten the integration with our AI features, apply some tweaks and fixes based on feedback, and remove the beta sticker. But as these agents evolved, simply provisioning a database wasn’t enough to keep them from breaking things.
Instead, we took a different approach.
We learned a lesson which prompted us to more deeply rethink our approach and go beyond a few fixes and tweaks. We realized that for a database solution to be truly integrated, it can’t just be another layer glued on top. It must offer the exact same degree of safe, isolated experimentation that code builders have enjoyed for years.
Today, Netlify becomes the only platform that natively pairs Deploy Previews with completely isolated database branches.
Safe experimentation is key
If your integrated database only has a single environment — “production” — it will break. That’s pretty much a given if you work in a team. But it turns out to be very common even for a solo developer. While building, you or an AI agent will make changes that break the live site. Some other changes you may end up wanting to revert, but rolling back database changes is often a special kind of nightmare.
In some other tools, there’s a built-in concept of a development database vs. a production one. That’s better, but still leaves a lot to be desired:
- How do you manage iterating on multiple features at the same time? Some you want and some you end up shelving.
- What happens if there’s a team involved? How do you untangle sticky conflicts?
Development teams have struggled with all of this as well. Many teams still have a few shared environments (often known as “staging” or “QA”) that are in a continually semi-broken state. Every working day, someone pushes their half-baked work to the same place, then semi-randomly adds or modifies data to test it. The pain is real.
Modern databases do offer a solution: branching. But branching has long been a pro-level feature that one has to know exactly how to set up and use. It’s a raw feature, rather than a workflow.
We’re all expected to be 10x builders today (no stress!). In practical terms, that means trying out more things, building and experimenting more, parallelizing more. Tooling to democratize that is essential. To provide you with a real solution, we went back to first principles. We can’t just provision a database for you and set our AI agent loose. Rather, we have to provide a workflow which bakes in pro-level best practices by default, for everyone.
We’re not trying to re-invent industry standards. We’re sticking to Postgres. All the needed tooling already exists out there. Our job has been to bring it all together.
An opinionated workflow
Here’s the workflow, in concrete terms:
- Whenever you start an agent run with Netlify’s Agent Runners and select the agent of your choice (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini), the agent will first decide whether a database is needed. If so, it provisions one, similar to what any comparable platform would do.
- On Netlify, however, each agent run also automatically gets its own database branch. The new branch is based on the latest production data, but all schema or content changes are contained within this branch.
- As the agent runs and generates new code, it also updates the data schema as needed by generating migration files. These include SQL commands to alter the database structure to its desired form

items.article_text: for iPhone iPhone iPad Mac Vision Watch TV Search Today Games Apps Arcade Platform iPhone iPad Mac Vision Watch TV Lovable: Build Apps With AI Build Websites & Tools Fast Free · In‑App Purchases · Designed for iPad. Not verified for macOS. Share 110 Ratings 4.6 Age Rating 16+ Years Category Productivity Developer Lovable Labs Incorporated Language EN English Size 41.7 MB iPhone, iPad Lovable turns your ideas into real, working apps and websites. Just describe what you want to make, and Lovable builds it — design, logic, backend, and all. You don't need to know how to code. You don't need a technical co-founder. You don't need to wait months for a developer. If you can describe what you want, you can build it today. Millions of people are already using Lovable to create things they never thought they could. Founders have launched revenue-generating businesses. Marketing teams have shipped websites in an afternoon instead of waiting six weeks. Product managers bring working prototypes to meetings instead of slide decks. Here's how it works: open Lovable, describe your idea in plain language — or drop in a screenshot — and watch it come to life. Lovable generates a complete, working app with real code, beautiful design, and everything wired up. Then iterate by chatting: adjust the layout, add features, connect your payment system, refine until it's exactly right. When you're ready, deploy with one click. What people build on Lovable: - Web apps and mobile apps - Websites and landing pages - Internal tools and dashboards - SaaS products and marketplaces - Custom tools for their team It connects to the tools you already use — Stripe for payments, GitHub for code, Notion for docs, Shopify for e-commerce, Slack for notifications, and dozens more. Authentication, database, and hosting are included from the start. No setup, no configuration, no infrastructure decisions. Lovable was built from day one for people with ideas, not just engineers. That's why it feels different from other tools — simple from the first moment, with professional-quality results you'd be proud to share. Over 25 million projects have been built on the platform. items.description:
Build real products with AI in minutes.
Lovable is an AI software builder that turns your idea into working websites or web apps. No code required.

Create tools and websites fast using natural language. Whether you’re a founder, vibe coder or developer, Lovable enables you to ship apps quickly without coding knowledge.

With Lovable you can:
• Build software with AI
• Turn ideas into real products
• Create web apps
• Prototype ideas fast
• Build without coding or with full code control
• Develop tools to improve your teams productivity
• Vibe coding websites

Perfect for:
• Startup founders
• Hackathons & side projects
• Beginners learning app development
• Developers who want to ship faster
• Teams replacing SaaS tools or building their own
• Vibe coders

Why Lovable?
• AI-powered software builder
• No-code and low-code workflows
• Fast prototyping and instant previews
• Cloud-based, private, and secure
• Developer without needing engineers

Whether you’re experimenting with an idea or building your next startup, Lovable helps you move from concept to app faster. If you’ve used tools like Claude, ChatGPT, Perplexity or Gemini for coding, Lovable helps you go one step further — from prompts to complete, working applications. Describe what you want to build, and Lovable generates the structure, logic, and UI automatically.

Terms & policies: https://lovable.dev/terms

Lovable is an independent product and is not affiliated with ChatGPT, Claude, Replit, Cursor, Bolt, v0, Base44, Wix or Perplexity.

items.article_text:
Claude for Creative Work
Creative professionals look to technology to expand what's possible in their work. Claude can't replace taste or imagination, but it can open up new ways of working—faster and more ambitious ideation, a more expansive skill set, and the ability for creatives to take on larger-scale projects. AI can also help shoulder the parts of the creative process that eat up time by handling repetitive tasks and eliminating manual toil. Key to both these goals is integrating Claude into the tools the creative industry already knows and trusts.
Today, we’re releasing a set of connectors—tools that let Claude work alongside the software creative professionals rely on, so creatives can extend their reach.
Connecting Claude to creative tools
Connectors allow Claude to access other platforms and tools directly. We are adding several new connectors that are designed to make it easier to use Claude for creative work:
[Ableton](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.f513efcc-5c05-46a0-8db3-7a9ea0134a54/directory/connectors/ant.dir.gh.ableton.ableton-knowledge)grounds Claude’s answers in official product documentation for Live and Push.[Adobe for creativity](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.f513efcc-5c05-46a0-8db3-7a9ea0134a54/directory/connectors/adobe-creativity)enables users to bring images, videos, and designs to life, drawing from 50+ tools across Creative Cloud apps including Photoshop, Premiere, Express, and more.[Affinity by Canva](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.f513efcc-5c05-46a0-8db3-7a9ea0134a54/directory/connectors/ant.dir.gh.canva.affinity)automates repetitive production tasks across pro creative workflows - such as batch image adjustments, layer renaming, and file export - and generates custom features directly in the app.[Autodesk Fusion](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.f513efcc-5c05-46a0-8db3-7a9ea0134a54/directory/connectors/ant.dir.gh.autodesk.fusion-mcp)allows designers and engineers with a Fusion subscription to create and modify 3D models through conversations with Claude.[Blender](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.f513efcc-5c05-46a0-8db3-7a9ea0134a54/directory/connectors/ant.dir.gh.blender.blender-mcp)offers a natural-language interface to its Python API, allowing users to explore and understand complex setups and making it easier to access Blender’s documentation.[Resolume Arena](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.f513efcc-5c05-46a0-8db3-7a9ea0134a54/directory/connectors/ant.dir.gh.resolume.resolume-arena)and[Resolume Wire](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.f513efcc-5c05-46a0-8db3-7a9ea0134a54/directory/connectors/ant.dir.gh.resolume.resolume-wire)let VJs and live visual artists control Arena, Avenue, and Wire in real time through natural language for live performance and AV production.[SketchUp](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.f513efcc-5c05-46a0-8db3-7a9ea0134a54/directory/connectors/sketchup)turns a conversation with Claude into a starting point for 3D modeling—describe a room, a piece of furniture, or a site concept, then open it in SketchUp to refine.[Splice](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.f513efcc-5c05-46a0-8db3-7a9ea0134a54/directory/connectors/splice)gives music producers the ability to search its catalog of royalty-free samples from within Claude.
Using Claude for creative work
Here are a few of the ways Claude can be used for creative tasks:
- Learning and mastering creative tools: Claude can act as an on-demand tutor for complex software. You can ask it to explain a modifier stack, walk you through a synthesis technique, or demonstrate an unfamiliar feature, and it will show you how to use it.
- Extending tools with code: Claude Code can write scripts, plugins, and generative systems for the software you already use. You can ask it to build a custom shader, script a procedural animation, or generate parametric models, and it will produce documented code you can reuse and modify.
- Bridging tools in a pipeline: Claude can translate formats, restructure
