Workbench
Access and control your Mac from anywhere
Modern remote desktop for Mac, iPad, and iPhone
[Download](https://astropad.com/app-downloads/workbench/)
Try it free for 20 minutes per day
Remote desktop for the AI era,
not IT support
Interact with apps, navigate your desktop, and control your system with speed and responsiveness that feel natural.
Fast, high-fidelity streaming
- Perceptually lossless visuals
- Retina support
- Proprietary multi-codec system
Voice input control
- Dictate prompts and commands directly to your Mac from your iPhone or iPad
Built for Apple devices
- Workbench uses fully native apps built specifically for Mac, iPad, and iPhone
Your agents don’t stop
when you leave your desk
Running AI agents on a headless Mac mini? You still need to see what's happening.
Remote Workflows
Keep tabs on your AI agents
Babysit your agents from anywhere, without being tethered to your desk.
Monitor jobs in real time
Review logs and progress as your AI agents run behind the scenes. Spot issues, make changes, and restart automated jobs without being tethered to your desk.
Manage headless machines
Running your Mac mini as a dedicated AI computer? Use Workbench to check in remotely and make changes without connecting to additional monitors or peripherals.
Why Workbench
Designed for seamless remote control
"The easiest way to gain remote access to a Mac"
Here's what real people are saying about Workbench:
Using Workbench
Input that moves
as fast as your workflow
Control your Mac using the input methods you already rely on.
Apple Pencil support
Use Apple Pencil for precise cursor control when interacting with apps on your Mac.
Gestures
Use familiar gestures to zoom, pan, and navigate your Mac from your iPad or iPhone.
Middle mouse
Navigate 3D tools, CAD software, and other precision workflows using full middle mouse support.
Speech-to-text
Dictate text from your iPhone or iPad and send it directly to your Mac.
Powered by LIQUID
Blazing fast & crystal clear
Workbench is built on Astropad’s proprietary LIQUID technology — the same engine that powers Luna Display and Astropad Studio.
Ultra low latency
Interact with your Mac in real time with extremely low latency, even on high-resolution displays.
Vibrant colors
Display rich, vibrant content in full color without the heavy compression or clipping common in many remote desktop apps.
Velocity control
Maintain smooth zooming, panning, and scrolling as LIQUID adapts to changing network conditions.
Proprietary multi-codec system
Experience crisp visuals and responsive performance as LIQUID selects the optimal codec for each frame.
Retina support
Enjoy high-resolution visuals optimized for Retina and high-density displays.
Visually lossless
Get a clear view with clean visuals and minimal artifacts or clutter.
Seamless access, anywhere in the world
Built on a global relay network
Most remote desktop tools require router tweaks, firewall fixes, or fragile NAT tricks. Workbench just connects.
Our global relay network spans 11 regions and intelligently routes your session for fast, reliable performance anywhere in the world.
Connect instantly without port forwarding, complicated network configurations, or manual setup.
Locked down with end-to-end encryption
With end-to-end AES-256 encryption, your screensharing sessions stay completely private.
Sessions are transmitted securely, and no one, including us, can see your screen, keyboard, mouse, or any of your activity.
Workbench is designed from the ground up using modern security best practices so that your work and data stay protected.
Frequently Asked Questions
What are the system requirements for Workbench?
Workbench requires the following: - Mac: macOS 15 or later - iPhone: iOS 26 or later - iPad: iPadOS 26 or later Apple Silicon is strongly preferred. Intel Macs are supported but limited to the LIQUID codec only. H265 is not available on Intel hardware. Workbench does not support Windows.What are the recommended network settings fo

items.description: The content details the use of Agent CI, a tool for running GitHub Actions locally. It emphasizes the speed and efficiency of Agent CI by leveraging local resources for caching, allowing for immediate feedback on CI processes. Key principles include pausing on failure to enable debugging without the need to push changes, and ensuring compatibility with the official GitHub Actions runner. An architectural comparison highlights Agent CI's advantages over other local runners and the traditional cloud CI approach. Additionally, the document includes user testimonials praising Agent CI's functionality and a quick start guide for installation and use, particularly for AI agent integration.
items.description_zh: 该内容详细介绍了 Agent CI 的使用方法，这是一款用于在本地运行 GitHub Actions 的工具。它通过利用本地资源进行缓存，强调 Agent CI 的速度和效率，能够即时反馈 CI 流程。核心原则包括：在失败时暂停以便调试，无需推送更改；确保与官方 GitHub Actions 运行器的兼容性。架构对比突出了 Agent CI 相较于其他本地运行器和传统云端 CI 方法的优势。此外，文档还包含用户对 Agent CI 功能的好评，以及快速入门指南（涵盖安装和使用，特别是针对 AI 代理集成）。

Add any item you’re considering, set a cooldown timer, and let the urge settle before taking action. When check-in time arrives, Introspect guides you through short reflection cards that help uncover the real reasons behind wanting to buy. items.description_zh:
Introspect 是一款专注理性消费的陪伴工具，旨在帮助你暂停冲动购物，理解购买背后的动机，在消费前做出更清醒的决策。

添加任何你心仪的商品，设置冷静计时器，让冲动情绪平复后再行动。当检查时间到来时，Introspect 会引导你完成简短的反思卡片，帮助你挖掘购买冲动背后的真实原因。随后你将选择最终结果，逐步建立个人决策历史记录，见证更明智的消费选择。

核心功能：

- 冷静计时器：为计划中的购买设置暂停期
- 引导式检查：通过滑动式反思卡片进行自我对话
- 洞察卡片：揭示每次购买冲动背后的真实动机
- 决策收据：总结你的决策结果与关键洞察
- 决策结果与活动追踪
- 钱包式时间线：记录消费决策与支出模式
- 每周实用技巧：减少冲动消费
- 14天现实核查：回顾过往决策，发现消费模式
- 可选好友验证：购买前获取第二意见

items.article_text:
Project Glasswing
Securing critical software for the AI era
Introduction
Today we’re announcing Project Glasswing1, a new initiative that brings together Amazon Web Services, Anthropic, Apple, Broadcom, Cisco, CrowdStrike, Google, JPMorganChase, the Linux Foundation, Microsoft, NVIDIA, and Palo Alto Networks in an effort to secure the world’s most critical software.
We formed Project Glasswing because of capabilities we’ve observed in a new frontier model trained by Anthropic that we believe could reshape cybersecurity. Claude Mythos2 Preview is a general-purpose, unreleased frontier model that reveals a stark fact: AI models have reached a level of coding capability where they can surpass all but the most skilled humans at finding and exploiting software vulnerabilities.
Mythos Preview has already found thousands of high-severity vulnerabilities, including some in every major operating system and web browser. Given the rate of AI progress, it will not be long before such capabilities proliferate, potentially beyond actors who are committed to deploying them safely. The fallout—for economies, public safety, and national security—could be severe. Project Glasswing is an urgent attempt to put these capabilities to work for defensive purposes.
As part of Project Glasswing, the launch partners listed above will use Mythos Preview as part of their defensive security work; Anthropic will share what we learn so the whole industry can benefit. We have also extended access to a group of over 40 additional organizations that build or maintain critical software infrastructure so they can use the model to scan and secure both first-party and open-source systems. Anthropic is committing up to $100M in usage credits for Mythos Preview across these efforts, as well as $4M in direct donations to open-source security organizations.
Project Glasswing is a starting point. No one organization can solve these cybersecurity problems alone: frontier AI developers, other software companies, security researchers, open-source maintainers, and governments across the world all have essential roles to play. The work of defending the world’s cyber infrastructure might take years; frontier AI capabilities are likely to advance substantially over just the next few months. For cyber defenders to come out ahead, we need to act now.
Cybersecurity in the age of AI
The software that all of us rely on every day—responsible for running banking systems, storing medical records, linking up logistics networks, keeping power grids functioning, and much more—has always contained bugs. Many are minor, but some are serious security flaws that, if discovered, could allow cyberattackers to hijack systems, disrupt operations, or steal data.
We have already seen the serious consequences of cyberattacks for important [corporate networks](https://cloud.google.com/blog/topics/threat-intelligence/oracle-ebusiness-suite-zero-day-exploitation), [healthcare systems](https://www.nao.org.uk/reports/investigation-wannacry-cyber-attack-and-the-nhs/), [energy infrastructure](https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/news/attack-colonial-pipeline-what-weve-learned-what-weve-done-over-past-two-years), [transport hubs](https://www.lawfaremedia.org/article/lessons-from-the-european-airports-ransomware-attack), and the information security of [government](https://www.reuters.com/world/us/hackers-solarwinds-breach-stole-data-us-sanctions-policy-intelligence-probes-2021-10-07/) [agencies](https://www.reuters.com/technology/cybersecurity/us-treasurys-workstations-hacked-cyberattack-by-china-afp-reports-2024-12-30/) [across](https://lordslibrary.parliament.uk/cyber-security-and-the-uk-government/) the world. On the global stage, state-sponsored attacks from actors like China, Iran, North Korea, and Russia have threatened to compromise the infrastructure that underpins both civilian life and military readiness. Even smaller-scale attacks, such as those where individual [hospitals](https://www.sciencedirect

items.article_text:
Moving Railway's Frontend Off Next.js
Railway's entire production frontend no longer runs on Next.js. The dashboard, the canvas, [railway.com](http://railway.com/), all of it now runs on Vite + TanStack Router, and we shipped the migration in two PRs with zero downtime.
Next.js got [railway.com](http://railway.com/) from zero to a production app serving millions of users monthly. It's an excellent framework, but it stopped being the right one for our product.
Frontend builds had crept past 10 minutes. Six of those minutes were Next.js alone, half of it stuck on "finalizing page optimization." For a team that ships multiple times a day, that kind of build time isn't a minor annoyance. It's like a very expensive tax on every single iteration.
Railway’s app is overwhelmingly client-side. The dashboard is a rich, stateful interface. The canvas is real-time. Websockets are everywhere. The server-first primitives in Next.js weren't something we used, and we'd ended up building our own abstractions on top of the Pages Router just to support layouts and routing concerns that the framework didn't handle the way we needed.
We were still on the Pages Router, which made shared layouts hacky. Every layout pattern was a bolted-on workaround rather than a first-class framework primitive. The App Router would have solved some of these problems, but it leans heavily into server-first patterns, and our product is intentionally client-driven. Adopting it would have meant rebuilding around a paradigm we don't need.
We wanted a stack that matches how we actually build: explicit, client-first, and fast to iterate on. It also helps that we genuinely enjoy working with it.
For the Product team, we wanted a few niceties that help us avoid thinking about how we needed to implement our front-end and found the following to really convince us.
- Type-safe routing out of the box. Route params and search params are inferred, autocomplete works across the entire route tree, and the routes themselves are generated from the file system.
- First-class layouts. Pathless layout routes replaced all of our previous hacks with something composable and predictable.
- A dev loop fast enough that you stop thinking about it. Instant HMR, near-zero startup time. The feedback cycle between changing code and seeing the result effectively disappears.
- SSR where it actually matters. Marketing pages, the changelog, careers. Pure client-side everywhere else, because we're not going to force server rendering on screens that don't benefit from it. (Ed: This Blog, ironically isn’t on Tanstack yet.)
- An explicit model. Meaning, we found TanStack to lean less on framework magic, more control over how things actually work under the hood.
Several of us tried TanStack Start over the holidays and the reaction was unanimous. We like building with it, and for a product like Railway's dashboard, that matters as much as any benchmark.
Once we made the choice, I got to work. Pre-squash before merge, I must have made 100s of commits.
Migrating a production frontend that serves millions of users across 200+ routes is the kind of thing that usually takes months of parallel running and incremental cutover. We were on a deadline, so I did it in two pull requests.
PR 1 replaced everything Next.js-specific: next/image
, next/head
, next/router
. Each was swapped for either a native browser API or a framework-agnostic alternative. This PR changed nothing about the framework itself. It just removed every dependency on it, so that PR 2 could be a clean swap.
PR 2 swapped the framework. 200+ routes migrated. We systematically extracted everything non-routing-related from page files into individual React components first, then generated all routes from the original page tree.
We then added Nitro as the server layer and replaced next.config.js
with Nitro config, consolidating redirects (500+), security headers, and caching rules into one place. We also replaced Node.js APIs that Next.js had provided p

items.article_text:
We Found a Ticking Time Bomb in macOS TCP Networking - It Detonates After Exactly 49 Days
Every Mac has a hidden expiration date. After exactly 49 days, 17 hours, 2 minutes, and 47 seconds of continuous uptime, a 32-bit unsigned integer overflow in Apple's XNU kernel freezes the internal TCP timestamp clock. Once frozen, TIME_WAIT connections never expire, ephemeral ports slowly exhaust, and eventually no new TCP connections can be established at all. ICMP (ping) keeps working. Everything else dies. The only fix most people know is a reboot. We discovered this bug on our iMessage service monitoring fleet, reproduced it live on two machines, and traced the root cause to a single comparison in the XNU kernel source. This is the full story.
Background: The Concepts You Need
Before diving into the bug, here is a quick primer on the building blocks. If you already know what TIME_WAIT
, MSL, and integer overflow mean, skip ahead to the discovery.
What Is TIME_WAIT
?
When a TCP connection closes, it does not disappear immediately. The side that initiates the close enters a state called TIME_WAIT. In this state, the connection is functionally dead — no data flows — but the operating system keeps it around for a short period.
Why? Two reasons:
Late packets. The internet does not guarantee packet ordering. A packet from the old connection could still be bouncing through routers. If the OS immediately reused the same source port and destination for a new connection, that stale packet could be misinterpreted as belonging to the new connection, corrupting data.
Reliable close. TCP's four-way close handshake ends with a final ACK from the active closer. If that ACK gets lost, the other side will retransmit its FIN. The TIME_WAIT state keeps the connection alive long enough to handle that retransmission.
The duration of TIME_WAIT is defined as 2 × MSL (Maximum Segment Lifetime). Once it expires, the OS reclaims the connection's resources — including the ephemeral port it occupied.
What Is MSL?
MSL (Maximum Segment Lifetime) is the longest time a TCP segment is expected to survive in the network before being discarded. RFC 793, the original TCP specification from 1981, set MSL at 2 minutes, making TIME_WAIT = 4 minutes.
In practice, modern operating systems use much shorter values:
Operating System | MSL | TIME_WAIT duration (2×MSL) |
|---|---|---|
Linux | 30 seconds | 60 seconds |
macOS / XNU | 15 seconds | 30 seconds |
Windows | 120 seconds (default) | 240 seconds |
On macOS, a closed TCP connection sits in TIME_WAIT for just 30 seconds before being cleaned up. That is fast — unless the cleanup mechanism itself breaks.
What Is a 32-Bit Unsigned Integer Wraparound?
A uint32_t
in C can hold values from 0 to 4,294,967,295 (2³² − 1). When you try to store a value larger than that maximum, the number wraps around back to zero — like an odometer rolling over from 999,999 to 000,000.
This is not a crash or an error. It is defined behavior for unsigned integers in C. The danger is when code assumes the counter only goes up and does not account for the wraparound.
Famous examples of this class of bug:
Windows 95/98's 49.7-day crash — The kernel's 32-bit millisecond tick counter overflowed, and internal components did not handle the wraparound, causing the system to hang.
The Year 2038 Problem (Y2K38) — Unix systems that store time as a signed 32-bit integer (seconds since 1970) will overflow on January 19, 2038.
GPS Week Number Rollover — GPS uses a 10-bit week counter that overflows every 1,024 weeks (~19.7 years), causing some receivers to report incorrect dates.
Pac-Man's kill screen at level 256 — An 8-bit integer overflow made the game unwinnable past level 255.
The bug we found in macOS belongs to this exact family. The XNU kernel stores its TCP timestamp as a uint32_t
counting milliseconds since boot. 2³² milliseconds = 49 days, 17 hours, 2 minutes, and 47.296 seconds. After that, the counter wraps back to zero. What happens next is the subje

items.article_text: Menu NASA Artemis Redefining NASA’s Artemis programme to inspire the next generation of space discovery. Read more NASA is an organisation of great complexity. An agency with numerous programmes, missions, directorates, facilities, and technologies, partnering with other international space agencies, aerospace companies, and a vast array of commercial partners. Artemis is one of its key programmes. Humanity’s return to the moon, 50 years after Apollo, in a world facing new priorities. This time, it is to stay. Client NASA Artemis Expertise Brand Strategy Brand Identity Brand Experience Communications NASA’s Artemis programme, which will land the first woman and first person of colour on the moon, is tasked with exploring more of the lunar surface than ever before, in search of scientific discovery, technology advancements and to prepare for human missions to Mars. Artemis needed a brand strategy and identity that can tell a highly-technical story, over the best part of a decade, in a way old and new generations will emotionally connect with. Read more ‘New Dawn’ introduces this momentous new frontier of exploration by shifting the vantage point from earth to space, illuminating the path to new possibilities and shedding light on potential advancements for human life. Read more The 60º angle, which modernises the legacy NASA typeface and carves dimension and depth into communications, pays homage to the angle created when two planetary bodies are in optimal balance and harmony. And converses with the 0º and 90º angles that provide upward sensation. A more vivid colour palette elicits positivity and belief in the programmes ability to establish human presence on another planet, while photography captures the human stories, innovative spirit and determination fuelling our return to the moon and beyond. Read more The result is a storytelling universe that will drive engagement and conversation – highlighting the limits of human knowledge, and celebrating new achievements once again. Read more 00:00 About Address Contact

items.article_text:
The cost of building software has drastically decreased. We recently [rebuilt Next.js in one week](https://blog.cloudflare.com/vinext/) using AI coding agents. But for the past two months our agents have been working on an even more ambitious project: rebuilding the WordPress open source project from the ground up.
WordPress powers [over 40% of the Internet](https://w3techs.com/technologies/details/cm-wordpress). It is a massive success that has enabled anyone to be a publisher, and created a global community of WordPress developers. But the WordPress open source project will be 24 years old this year. Hosting a website has changed dramatically during that time. When WordPress was born, AWS EC2 didn’t exist. In the intervening years, that task has gone from renting virtual private servers, to uploading a JavaScript bundle to a globally distributed network at virtually no cost. It’s time to upgrade the most popular CMS on the Internet to take advantage of this change.
Our name for this new CMS is EmDash. We think of it as the spiritual successor to WordPress. It’s written entirely in TypeScript. It is serverless, but you can run it on your own hardware or any platform you choose. Plugins are securely sandboxed and can run in their own [isolate](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/reference/how-workers-works/), via [Dynamic Workers](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/runtime-apis/bindings/worker-loader/), solving the fundamental security problem with the WordPress plugin architecture. And under the hood, EmDash is powered by [Astro](https://astro.build/), the fastest web framework for content-driven websites.
EmDash is fully open source, MIT licensed, and [available on GitHub](https://github.com/emdash-cms/emdash). While EmDash aims to be compatible with WordPress functionality, no WordPress code was used to create EmDash. That allows us to license the open source project under the more permissive MIT license. We hope that allows more developers to adapt, extend, and participate in EmDash’s development.
You can deploy the EmDash v0.1.0 preview to your own Cloudflare account, or to any Node.js server today as part of our early developer beta:
Or you can try out the admin interface here in the [EmDash Playground](https://emdashcms.com/):
The story of WordPress is a triumph of open source that enabled publishing at a scale never before seen. Few projects have had the same recognisable impact on the generation raised on the Internet. The contributors to WordPress’s core, and its many thousands of plugin and theme developers have built a platform that democratised publishing for millions; many lives and livelihoods being transformed by this ubiquitous software.
There will always be a place for WordPress, but there is also a lot more space for the world of content publishing to grow. A decade ago, people picking up a keyboard universally learned to publish their blogs with WordPress. Today it’s just as likely that person picks up Astro, or another TypeScript framework to learn and build with. The ecosystem needs an option that empowers a wide audience, in the same way it needed WordPress 23 years ago.
EmDash is committed to building on what WordPress created: an open source publishing stack that anyone can install and use at little cost, while fixing the core problems that WordPress cannot solve.
WordPress’ plugin architecture is fundamentally insecure. [96% of security issues](https://patchstack.com/whitepaper/state-of-wordpress-security-in-2025/) for WordPress sites originate in plugins. In 2025, more high severity vulnerabilities [were found in the WordPress ecosystem](https://patchstack.com/whitepaper/state-of-wordpress-security-in-2026/) than the previous two years combined.
Why, after over two decades, is WordPress plugin security so problematic?
A WordPress plugin is a PHP script that hooks directly into WordPress to add or modify functionality. There is no isolation: a WordPress plugin has direct access to the Wor

items.description: Today we are launching the beta of EmDash, a full-stack serverless JavaScript CMS built on Astro 6.0. It combines the features of a traditional CMS with modern security, running plugins in sandboxed Worker isolates.

items.title: Introducing EmDash — the spiritual successor to WordPress that solves plugin security

items.article_text:
本文可算
[追求小屏智慧手機之路]的精神續篇?
近十數年我基本都帶著兩部手機出門, 但我對選購手機 (或者說, 手機形態的電子裝置?) 的標準一直都是 ｢能作為唯一主力使用｣, 這個挑選標準也確實在某些場合幫了大忙. 不過, ｢唯一主力｣ 也意味著必須滿足一些最低條件 ⸺ 總得有攝像頭用於識別無處不在的QR code, 最好有NFC便於搭乘公共交通/無接觸支付, 需要GPS以進行導航⋯⋯ 很多極具誘惑的裝置在套用這些條件後只能遺憾落選, 但我一直惦記著它們. 其中幾款尤其令我惦記的裝置都有個共同點: E-Ink. 與手機平板上常見的LCD/OLED不同, E-Ink成像原理更接近印刷品, 在陽光直射下依然清晰可讀, 靜止畫面無需耗電維持, 續航動輒以週計算, 而且對眼睛的負擔也遠比發光螢幕來得小 ⸺ 代價則是更新速度慢而不適合瀏覽動態內容, 以及絕大多數型號只能顯示灰階, 目前的彩色E-Ink面板基本都對比度低且顏色暗淡.
E-Ink手機在最近幾年似乎迎來了一次大流行, 從[detox手機](https://www.thelightphone.com/lightii)到[完全可擔當主力的旗艦機](https://cht.boox.com/products/palma2pro), 基本每個價位都有對應的E-Ink手機了. 但囿於 ｢唯一主力｣ 心態, 再加上对外觀的偏好, 這些手機 (還有[mudita](https://mudita.com/products/phones/mudita-kompakt/), [minimal](https://minimalcompany.com/)和[bigme](https://store.bigme.vip/)) 至多停留在令我惦記的程度.
會讓我開始不再將 ｢能作為唯一主力｣ 作為準則的契機其實還是reMarkable ⸺ 他們在去年九月發表了[reMarkable Paper Pro Move](https://remarkable.com/products/remarkable-paper/pro-move) (我很討厭這冗長的名稱, 以下簡稱Move), 一款7.3” 彩色E-Ink熒幕的便攜裝置. 和其他reMarkable產品一樣, Move主打順暢的書寫體驗, 從外到內都延續了reMarkable一貫的風格, 是一款十分令人心動的產品. 但它的價格也如reMarkable一貫的風格十分高昂, 令人望而卻步.
再仔細一想, 我自己日常使用的筆記本基本都是九口山Mini Note (130 × 75mm
) 或[Field Notes](https://fieldnotesbrand.com/products/original-kraft) (140 × 89mm
) 尺寸, Move (195.6 × 107.8mm
)顯著超大了. 雖然依然眼饞它精美的設計, 但考慮到高昂的售價以及彩色E-Ink糟糕的顯示效果, 我還是成功打消了購買Move的念頭 ⸺ 即便如此, 我的 ｢唯一主力｣ 心態受到了很大的動搖.
沒過兩個月, viwoods就發表了這臺[AiPaper Reader](https://viwoods.com/products/viwoods-aipaper-reader) (比起reMarkable Paper Pro Move, 我更討厭這個名字, 所以下文我會將其簡稱為viwoods而不是AiPaper). 和Move不同, 這臺裝置主打的是閱讀而非書寫, 價格與reMarkable也完全不是一個檔次. 雖然我非常討厭這個名字以及该產品圍繞AI進行的宣發, 但却不得不承認拜这AI功能所賜, viwoods成為了一部非常好的便攜閱讀裝置. 經歷過Move的動搖, 我還是購入了這臺頂多能算 ｢80%唯一主力｣ 的副機.
f/2.8
130s
ISO 100
40 mm
Viwoods擁有一塊6.13” 1648 × 824 (300 PPI), Carta 1300 (撰文时最高階之單色E-Ink面板) E-Ink熒幕, 機身尺寸159.39 × 80.27 × 7mm
, 比field note和mini note略長, 但依舊算非常口袋友好. 它的設計風格与reMarkable相似, 不過後蓋是塑膠而非玻璃. 它的手感同樣很好, 鋁合金中框与前後面板都做了細膩的磨砂處理, 中框的弧面也非常贴手. Viwoods設計上令我欣賞之處是它的中框採用了青鈍色, 集成了指紋識別的電源鍵則是明亮的鉛丹色, 给略嫌無聊的直板設計添了一抹亮色.
f/2
1125s
ISO 100
40 mm
雖然在宣傳上並沒有被視作手機, 但viwoods確實可以接打電話和收發簡訊, 只不過它沒有內建揚聲器与麥克風, 必須搭配藍芽耳機使用. 放棄了揚聲器与麥克風的另一個好處就是它的正面極端簡潔, 沒有任何醜陋的開孔.
f/2
150s
ISO 100
65 mm
Viwoods雖缺乏攝像頭, NFC, GPS而無法完全替代我的主力行動電話, 但作為便攜閱讀裝置的體驗卻是頂級 ⸺ 它具備SIM插槽与通話功能, 雖然仅支援4G, 但應付日常通訊綽綽有餘. 它搭載的Carta 1300型電子墨水熒幕支援快速更新模式, 而預置的Android 16也加入了了一些針對電子墨水熒幕的最佳化, 如可以針對不同的應用設定更新率, 自動殘影移除等, 即便偶爾需要以捲動而非翻頁的方式檢視, 體驗也勉強堪用. 若是在行程固定的使用場景 (AKA 社畜通勤) 下, 它甚至可以勉強作為唯一隨身裝置使用. 它的電池容量僅有2580mAh, 但如果僅用於通勤閱讀和聽播客音樂, 撑个一週問題不大; 若是当主力機那般高強度使用, 兩天左右電量便會耗盡. 它甚至在USB口旁有一枚LED指示燈, 可惜目前這個指示燈僅能指示充電狀態, 不能當作通知燈使用. (但妳真的希望這臺裝置能提示通知么⋯⋯?)
f/2.8
160s
ISO 100
40 mm
自帶移動網路的最大優勢在通勤閱讀這一場景下可以說被發揮至最大: 當我需要搭地鐵通勤时, 若能找到座位, viwoods可以抓取RSS/Podcast更新讓我閱讀最新部落格同時聆聽最新播客, 也可以透過Apple Music等流播服務串流音樂, 甚至不僅RSS, 我還可以利用[Mihon](https://github.com/mihonapp/mihon)閱讀漫畫新章⋯⋯
作為針對移動閱讀場景開發的裝置, 它的閱讀體驗可比[reMarkable 2](https://novadng.studio/2021-03/reMarkable-2/)要不知好到哪裡去了. 首先viwoods在中日韓文字支援方面就完勝 (畢竟是Android), 無需每次更新完都ssh加回CJK字型; 其次它可以安裝Android上各種強勁的閱讀應用, 雲同步什麼的都不在話下, 更能輕鬆做到針對性自定義epub閱讀體驗. 不過, 畢竟熒幕尺寸擺在那裡, 閱讀論文PDF的體驗確實普普.
作為軟體部分的收尾姑且提一嘴作為名字一部分和最大賣點但我絲毫不在意的AI功能 ⸺ viwoods在翻頁鍵下方有一顆專用AI按鈕, 按下即可喚起它自帶的AI hub, 其中集合了Qwen, Gemini, ChatGPT和Deepseek, 而且還會隨著系統更新而增加. 可惜這顆AI按鈕不能方便地自定義, 著實雞肋 ⸺ 我在上面裝了官方Claude應用来應付一些日常AI使用需求, 若是能自定義按鈕改成喚起Claude應用就好了.
f/4
130s
ISO 100
65 mm
我對viwoods硬體上唯一的抱怨在將它与reMarkable並排時最為明顯: 它的熒幕与保護层之間的空隙非常明顯, 在光線角度不好時熒幕邊框產生的陰影甚至會讓處於邊緣的文字變得難以閱讀. 當然, 絕大多數閱讀狀況下妳不會用到那麼小的邊距, 但這種情況的出現總是令人感到不悅, 例圖里因為開啟了裁切邊緣空白以獲得最大閱讀空間, 一些對話方塊中的文字就被擠到了會受到陰影影響的邊緣.
儘管viwoods已經算是很小巧了, 但若我着夏裝不帶包出門, 身上還是很難找到第二个足夠大的口袋来裝下它. 當然, 在這種狀況下還想著要攜帶一臺eink裝置專門負責閱讀的確是有點 ｢創造不存在的問題然後解決｣ 的意思, 但作為小裝置愛好者的我在看到[XTEink X4](https://www.xteink.com/products/xteink-x4) (閱星瞳X4, 下文简称X4) 时還是很難移開目光. 這個裝置114 × 69 × 5.9mm
的尺寸比field note与mini note都小, 可以完美滑入我大部分襯衫的胸口袋而不嫌詭異, 似乎是一臺完美的隨身文字閱讀裝置. 更重要的是它價格相當親民 (當我寫作本文時250人民幣, 63USD), 利好衝動消費 (當然, 如果妳之前就看過[微雪](https://www.waveshare.net/left_column/e-Paper.htm)之類的E-Ink螢幕供應商就不會衝動了) ⸺ 但千萬不要衝動, 妳馬上就會知道这背後是怎樣的代價⋯⋯
f/2.8
130s
ISO 100
35 mm
X4和viwoods可以說是便攜閱讀哲學的兩個極端 ⸺ viwoods作為加法派的代表, 與智慧手機相比已經相差無幾; X4 則能算是減法派, 砍掉任何與閱讀無關的功能, 回歸初代Kindle那般純粹甚至有些簡陋的體驗, 這點從X4的螢幕不支援觸摸和背光便得以清

items.description: 本文可算追求小屏智慧手機之路的精神續篇?   近十數年我基本都帶著兩部手機出門, 但我對選購手機 (或者說, 手機形態的電子裝置?) 的標準一直都是 ｢能作為唯一主力使用｣, 這個挑選標準也確實在某些場合幫了大忙. 不過, ｢唯一主力｣ 也意味著必須滿足一些最低條件 ⸺ 總得有攝像頭用於識別無處不在

items.article_text:
Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
Today, we are introducing [Gemma 4](https://aistudio.google.com/prompts/new_chat?model=gemma-4-31b-it) — our most intelligent open models to date. Purpose-built for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, Gemma 4 delivers an unprecedented level of intelligence-per-parameter. This breakthrough builds on incredible community momentum: since the launch of our first generation, developers have downloaded Gemma over 400 million times, building a vibrant [Gemmaverse](https://deepmind.google/models/gemma/gemmaverse/) of more than 100,000 variants. We listened closely to what innovators need next to push the boundaries of AI, and Gemma 4 is our answer: breakthrough capabilities made widely accessible under an [Apache 2.0 license](https://goo.gle/gemma-4-apache-2).
Open model performance vs size on [Arena.ai](http://arena.ai/)’s chat arena as of 4/1.
Built from the same world-class research and technology as Gemini 3, Gemma 4 is the most capable model family you can run on your hardware. They complement our Gemini models, giving developers the industry's most powerful combination of both open and proprietary tools.
Industry-leading capabilities and mobile-first AI
We are releasing Gemma 4 in four versatile sizes: [Effective 2B (E2B](https://huggingface.co/gg-hf-gg/gemma-4-E2B-it)), [Effective 4B (E4B)](https://huggingface.co/gg-hf-gg/gemma-4-E4B-it), [26B Mixture of Experts (MoE)](https://huggingface.co/gg-hf-gg/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it) and [31B Dense](https://huggingface.co/gg-hf-gg/gemma-4-31B-it). The entire family moves beyond simple chat to handle complex logic and agentic workflows. Our larger models deliver state-of-the-art performance for their sizes, with the 31B model currently ranking as the #3 open model in the world on the industry-standard [Arena AI text leaderboard](https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text?license=open-source), and the 26B model securing the #6 spot. There, Gemma 4 outcompetes models 20x its size. For developers, this new level of intelligence-per-parameter means achieving frontier-level capabilities with significantly less hardware overhead.
At the edge, our E2B and E4B models redefine on-device utility, prioritizing multimodal capabilities, low-latency processing and seamless ecosystem integration over raw parameter count.
Powerful, accessible, open
To power the next generation of pioneering research and products, we've sized the Gemma 4 models specifically to run and fine-tune efficiently on hardware — from billions of Android devices worldwide, to laptop GPUs, all the way up to developer workstations and accelerators.
By using these highly optimized models, you can fine-tune Gemma 4 to achieve state-of-the-art performance on your specific tasks. We've already seen incredible success with this approach; for instance, INSAIT created a pioneering Bulgarian-first language model ([BgGPT](https://deepmind.google/models/gemma/gemmaverse/insait/)), and we worked with Yale University on [Cell2Sentence-Scale](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/products/google-gemma-ai-cancer-therapy-discovery/) to discover new pathways for cancer therapy, among many others.
Here is what makes Gemma 4 our most capable open model family yet:
- Advanced reasoning: Capable of multi-step planning and deep logic, Gemma 4 demonstrates significant improvements in math and instruction-following benchmarks that require it.
- Agentic workflows: Native support for function-calling, structured JSON output, and native system instructions enables you to build autonomous agents that can interact with different tools and APIs and execute workflows reliably.
- Code generation: Gemma 4 supports high-quality offline code, turning your workstation into a local-first AI code assistant.
- Vision and audio: All models natively process video and images, supporting variable resolutions, and excelling at visual tasks like OCR and chart understanding. Additionally, the E2B and E4B models feature native audio

items.article_text:
Convex now has nearly 10,000 paying teams. Some showed up last month and deployed their first project in an afternoon. Others have been with us for years and have grown into businesses with millions of ARR.
But as these teams have grown, and recently, as bigger companies have started migrating onto Convex, we've heard a consistent message: "We love the product. Now we need the enterprise stuff." Dedicated infrastructure. SLAs. SSO. Compliance reports. Custom contracts.
So today we're launching [Convex for Enterprise](https://convex.dev/enterprise?ref=news.convex.dev), and offering two new plans for teams with scale. And while we're at it, we're making updates to our self-serve plans that will make them better—and cheaper for most of you.
Convex for Enterprise
Over the last six months, we've worked closely with some of our customers to design enterprise plans that actually address the needs they've had.
Here's what's in the box:
- Dedicated, high-scale deployments alongside the existing serverless options on today's plans. If you need guaranteed capacity and isolation, it's there.
- Unlimited deployments with flexible configurations—staging, multi-prod, whatever your team's workflow demands.
- CPU-time billing for actions and cache hit cost savings, so you're paying for what you actually use.
- SSO and SIEM-compatible audit logging, because your security team is going to ask.
- Fine-grained deployment access policies, so the intern can't accidentally nuke prod or leak PHI. (Or at least, not without the right permissions.)
- Slack connect, priority support with response time guarantees and service SLAs.
- Compliance reports and all the other stuff your procurement team needs to see before they'll let you use anything.
We have two enterprise tiers:
Business Plan — Month-to-month pricing with all enterprise features. $2,500/month minimum, no annual commitment required. If you need serious capabilities but want flexibility and don't yet have lawyers who love paperwork, this is it. This is often the Goldilocks zone for rapidly growing startups.
Enterprise Plan — Everything in Business, plus custom enterprise agreements, precommit discounts on annual contracts, and the kind of bespoke arrangement larger organizations typically need. Bigger companies migrating a second (or fourth) generation system onto Convex tend to prefer this way of doing business. We have no preference!
Convex enterprise plans are already powering innovative companies like [Conduit](https://conduit.ai/?ref=news.convex.dev), [doxy.me](https://doxy.me/?ref=news.convex.dev), [Orchid](https://orchid.ai/?ref=news.convex.dev), [Reducto](https://reducto.ai/?ref=news.convex.dev), and [Town](https://www.town.com/?ref=news.convex.dev).
If you're interested in learning more, reach out to [sales@convex.dev](mailto:sales@convex.dev). We can move your existing team onto an enterprise plan and upgrade your project's production to a dedicated deployment with zero downtime.
Updates to Free, Starter, and Pro
While enterprise is the headline today, this section likely interests more of you. After all, the vast majority of our customers are on these plans and are happy to stay there.
We first launched our current self-serve plans in December 2022. In over three years, we've made essentially no major changes to them, other than adding vector indexing and introducing the scale-to-zero Starter plan. We've learned a lot about how our business works in the meantime, and the good news is: we're pretty damn happy with how these plans have held up. The prices are fair, work very well for most of our customers and our business, and don't need any major changes.
But we're making some small tweaks because we got a few things wrong. But don't worry: for most users, these changes will actually lower your Convex bill and give you more built-in resources (we've done the math). Here's the rundown.
Deployments: 120 → 300
While we're proud to offer 120 deployments on the Pro plan today, in the agenti

[Post](https://x.com/densumesh/status/2039765361533637016/compose/post)

![Cheng](https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_images/378800000010252572/78d17364b6d52a63220206d139e6ebbd_normal.jpeg)

Cheng

@chumsdock

## Article

See new posts

# Conversation

[![](https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_images/1823803852325564416/aLyrvaLb_normal.jpg)](https://x.com/densumesh/status/2039765361533637016/densumesh)

[Dens Sumesh![](https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_images/2007938629797310464/XmtD5CZz_bigger.jpg)](https://x.com/densumesh/status/2039765361533637016/densumesh)

[@densumesh](https://x.com/densumesh/status/2039765361533637016/densumesh)

[![Image](https://pbs.twimg.com/media/HE3NSrYbIAAVhm8?format=jpg&name=small)](https://x.com/densumesh/status/2039765361533637016/densumesh/article/2039765361533637016/media/2039511927035207680)

Building a Virtual Filesystem for Mintlify's AI Assistant

47

154

1.1K

[920K](https://x.com/densumesh/status/2039765361533637016/densumesh/status/2039765361533637016/analytics)

RAG is great, until it isn't.

Our assistant could only retrieve chunks of text that matched a query. If the answer lived across multiple pages, or the user needed exact syntax that didn't land in a top-K result, it was stuck. We wanted it to explore docs the way you'd explore a codebase.

Agents are 

[converging on filesystems as their primary interface](https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.11672)

 because grep, cat, ls, and find are all an agent needs. If each doc page is a file and each section is a directory, the agent can search for exact strings, read full pages, and traverse the structure on its own. We just needed a filesystem that mirrored the live docs site.

## 

The Container Bottleneck

The obvious way to do this is to just give the agent a real filesystem. Most harnesses solve this by spinning up an isolated sandbox and cloning the repo. We already use sandboxes for asynchronous background agents where latency is an afterthought, but for a frontend assistant where a user is staring at a loading spinner, the approach falls apart. Our p90 session creation time (including GitHub clone and other setup) was \~46 seconds.

Beyond latency, dedicated micro-VMs for reading static documentation introduced a serious infrastructure bill.

At 850,000 conversations a month, even a minimal setup (1 vCPU, 2 GiB RAM, 5-minute session lifetime) would put us north of $70,000 a year based on 

[Daytona's per-second sandbox pricing](https://www.daytona.io/pricing)

 ($0.0504/h per vCPU, $0.0162/h per GiB RAM). Longer session times double that. (This is based on a purely naive approach, a true production workflow would probably have warm pools and container sharing, but the point still stands)

We needed the filesystem workflow to be instant and cheap, which meant rethinking the filesystem itself.

## 

Faking a Shell

The agent doesn't need a real filesystem; it just needs the illusion of one. Our documentation was already indexed, chunked, and stored in a Chroma database to power our search, so we built ChromaFs: a virtual filesystem that intercepts UNIX commands and tra
items.description:
RAG is great, until it isn't.
Our assistant could only retrieve chunks of text that matched a query. If the answer lived across multiple pages, or the user needed exact syntax that didn't land in a top-K result, it was stuck. We wanted it to explore docs the way you'd explore a codebase.
items.description_zh:
RAG 虽好，但并非万能。
我们的助手只能检索与查询匹配的文本片段。如果答案分散在多个页面中，或者用户需要的精确语法不在前 K 个结果中，它就会卡住。我们希望它能像你探索代码库一样探索文档。
items.title: Building a Virtual Filesystem for Mintlify's AI Assistant
items.title_zh: 为 Mintlify 的 AI 助手构建虚拟文件系统

items.description:
How we use AI at HEX

A few weeks ago, we launched a new brand for Cotool (cotool.ai), an agentic security platform. Half of the assets on their site were generated by a tool we built ourselves: a vibe-coded web app that maps any image or text onto the dithering system we designed. We made it in a day, and spent another few days tweaking, testing, and expanding.
items.description_zh:
HEX 如何运用AI

几周前，我们为Cotool（cotool.ai）推出了全新品牌——这是一个自主安全平台。该网站上一半的素材都出自我们自建的工具：一个通过"氛围编程"开发的网页应用，能将任意图像或文本映射到我们设计的抖动系统上。我们仅用一天就完成了开发，随后又花了几天时间进行调试、测试和功能扩展。

items.article_text:
If you already use Claude Code, this Codex plugin gives you a simple way to pull Codex into the same workflow.
It is useful for three things: a normal Codex review, a more skeptical adversarial review, and handing work off to Codex when you want a second pass from a different agent.
Setup
 
Check out the plugin here: https://github.com/openai/codex-plugin-cc

You need a ChatGPT subscription, including Free, or an OpenAI API key, plus Node.js 18.18 or later.
If Codex is not installed yet:
 
If Codex is installed but not authenticated:
 
Core commands
/codex:review runs the standard read-only Codex review.
/codex:adversarial-review runs a steerable challenge review. Use it when you want Codex to question the implementation, not just inspect it.
/codex:rescue hands a task to Codex directly.
Longer jobs can run in the background, with /codex:status, /codex:result, and /codex:cancel to manage them.
A simple first run:
When to use it
 
The main value here is getting a real second pass from a different agent without leaving Claude Code.
A good default pattern is simple:
Run /codex:review for all things
Run /codex:adversarial-review on anything high stakes
Use /codex:rescue when a thread stalls or you want Codex to take over
Adversarial review is especially useful for migrations, auth changes, infra scripts, refactors, and anything where the danger is hidden assumptions rather than obvious syntax mistakes.
How it works
The plugin delegates through the local Codex CLI and Codex app server. So it uses the same local auth, config, environment, and MCP setup you already have with Codex.
That is why it feels lightweight. It is not a separate runtime. It is Codex, just invoked from inside Claude Code.
Optional review gate
You can also enable a review gate:
 
That can stop Claude Code from exiting before a Codex review has run.
Useful, but something to use carefully. It can create a long Claude/Codex loop and burn through usage limits quickly.
Bottom line
This plugin is a simple way to keep your Claude Code workflow and still use Codex where Codex is strong.
Install it, run /codex:setup, use /codex:review as your default second pass, and reach for /codex:adversarial-review or /codex:rescue when you want more than a routine review.
Check out the plugin here: https://github.com/openai/codex-plugin-cc

items.description: The 'History of Software Design' is an upcoming interactive exhibit that aims to celebrate influential software designs and their creators. It addresses the lack of accessible documentation on software history and intends to restore significant interfaces and interactions, like the Xerox Alto and 'Slide to Unlock'. The project acknowledges the vastness of software history and aims for a selective approach, focusing on diverse impactful moments. Restoration efforts are challenged by low-quality assets, requiring reverse engineering to capture original details.
items.description_zh: 《软件设计史》是一个即将推出的互动展览，旨在致敬具有影响力的软件设计及其创作者。该项目致力于弥补软件历史领域缺乏易获取文献的现状，计划重现诸如施乐奥托（Xerox Alto）和"滑动解锁"（Slide to Unlock）等标志性界面与交互方式。项目团队认识到软件历史的浩瀚无垠，将采取选择性策略，聚焦于多元化的高光时刻。由于原始素材质量参差不齐，复原工作面临挑战，需通过逆向工程捕捉原始设计细节。
items.title: History of Software Design
items.title_zh: 软件设计史

items.description: The webpage discusses the use of cookies, explaining that they are small information pieces sent by websites to recognize users and improve their experience without identifying them personally. It highlights a new typeface, 'Verbo,' characterized by a blend of geometric precision and warm aesthetics, inspired by Bauhaus and Art Deco styles. The Verbo family is versatile for corporate design, signage, and packaging, while the 'Verbo XL' variant offers a large x-height and alternate characters influenced by historical styles. Links to various versions of the Verbo typeface, including Cyrillic, Greek, Arabic, and Hebrew, are provided.
items.description_zh: 该网页讨论了Cookie的使用，解释其是网站发送的小型信息片段，用于识别用户并改善体验，但不会识别个人身份。网页重点介绍了一款名为"Verbo"的新字体，其特点融合了几何精确性与温暖美学，灵感源自包豪斯和装饰艺术风格。Verbo字体家族适用于企业设计、标牌和包装，而"Verbo XL"变体则提供大x高度和受历史风格影响的替代字符。页面提供了Verbo字体的多个版本链接，包括西里尔字母、希腊字母、阿拉伯字母和希伯来字母版本。
items.title: Verbo | DSType Foundry
items.title_zh: Verbo | DSType Foundry

items.article_text:
Agents, meet the Figma canvas
Starting today, you can use AI agents to design directly on the Figma canvas. And with skills, you can guide agents with context about your team’s decisions and intent.
Share Agents, meet the Figma canvas
We're quickly improving how Figma supports AI agents. This will eventually be a usage-based paid feature, but is currently available for free during the beta period.
Design decisions—from color palettes and button padding, to typography and interactivity—have always defined how products take shape. No matter how small, those decisions add up. They make your product and user experience stand out from the rest. To date, AI agents haven't had this context, which is why so many designs created by AI often feel unfamiliar and generic.
With [Figma’s MCP server](https://www.figma.com/blog/introducing-figma-mcp-server/), agents can now write directly to your Figma files, extending the standards you’ve carefully established over time. Via the
Introducing our MCP server: Bringing Figma into your workflow
Today we’re announcing the beta release of the Figma MCP server, which brings Figma directly into the developer workflow to help LLMs achieve design-informed code generation.
use_figma
tool, Claude Code, Codex, and other MCP clients can generate and modify design assets that are linked to your design system. No matter where product work starts—with a coding agent, in Figma, or from the command line—Figma is where it all comes into focus. "Teams at OpenAI use Figma to iterate, refine, and make decisions about how a product comes together," says Ed Bayes, design lead at Codex. "Now, Codex can find and use all the important design context in Figma to help us build higher quality products more efficiently.”You can move fluidly between code and canvas, with agents operating in the same shared context. And skills—a set of instructions, written as markdown files—now directly shape how agents build on the Figma canvas. That expertise guides every piece of output and is more valuable now than ever.
[Instruct your agents with skills](https://www.figma.com#instruct-your-agents-with-skills)
Working across code and canvas
Our existing [ generate_figma_design tool ](https://www.figma.com/blog/introducing-claude-code-to-figma/)translates HTML from live apps and websites into editable Figma layers. Our new
use_figma
tool empowers agents to operate on the canvas using your design system, making this a step forward for agent workflows in Figma.These tools are complementary. When designs fall out of sync with code, generate_figma_design
brings the latest UI into Figma to iterate. From there, use_figma
can edit those designs—or create new assets—using your components and variables.
Opening the canvas to AI agents unlocks access to your design systems and files. Skills define how agents use that access. They outline how a workflow is executed in Figma: which steps to take, what sequencing to follow, and which conventions to abide by. But beyond the workflow itself, skills ensure that agents have the specialized knowledge and context they need to produce durable, brand-aligned designs—and know what good looks like. They bridge the knowledge gap so agents know how to work in Figma. "The best products come from teams who care deeply about the details," says Cat Wu, head of product for Claude Code. "Many design teams shape their work in Figma and bring those products to life with Claude Code. Skills teach Claude Code how to work directly in the design canvas, so you can build in a way that stays true to your team’s intent and judgment."
Anyone can author a skill and writing one doesn’t require building a plugin or writing code. Several of the skills launching today come from practitioners in the community who are building these workflows and defining what great design looks like in practice. At the core is a foundational Figma skill—/figma-use
—which all other skills build on. It gives agents a shared understanding of how Figma wo
items.article_title_extracted: Agents, Meet the Figma Canvas | Figma Blog

items.description:
Playbit is a software platform designed for creating playful personal-scale applications. It enables users to build apps tailored to them and their friends, overcoming limitations of traditional web-based applications. The initial release supports macOS, with future plans for cross-platform functionality across desktop, web, and mobile environments.

Key Features:
- **Playbit Runtime**: Acts like a minimal OS, providing a stable syscall interface for developing dynamic graphical apps.
- **Collaborative Development**: Focuses on multimedia and collaborative applications that can run across different platforms without needing multiple rebuilds.
- **User-Friendly Development**: Encourages quick prototyping and gradual complexity, allowing developers to start simple and expand later.
- **Object-Based Security**: Employs handle-based security for managing capabilities, enhancing fault isolation and sandboxing.
- **Comprehensive Toolset**: Aims to support the full app development lifecycle, including debugging, profiling, and collaboration tools.

The platform aspires to make app development simpler and more enjoyable, addressing the challenges of building cross-platform applications.
items.description_zh:
Playbit 是一个专为创建趣味性个人级应用而设计的软件平台。它让用户能够构建适合自己和朋友的应用程序，突破了传统网页应用的局限性。首个版本支持 macOS 系统，未来计划实现跨桌面端、网页端和移动端的全平台功能。

核心特性：
- **Playbit 运行时**：类似微型操作系统，提供稳定的系统调用接口，用于开发动态图形应用。
- **协作开发**：专注于多媒体和协作类应用，无需多次重构即可跨平台运行。
- **用户友好开发**：支持快速原型设计和渐进式复杂度扩展，开发者可从简单起步，后续逐步完善。
- **基于对象的安全机制**：采用句柄式安全管理能力，增强故障隔离和沙箱防护。
- **完整工具链**：旨在覆盖应用开发全生命周期，包含调试、性能分析和协作工具。

该平台致力于让应用开发更简单、更有趣，解决跨平台应用构建中的核心挑战。
items.title: playbit

items.article_text:
GPT-5.4 is a better web developer than its predecessors—generating more visually appealing and ambitious frontends. Notably, we trained GPT-5.4 with a focus on improved UI capabilities and use of images. With the right guidance, the model can produce production-ready frontends incorporating subtle touches, well-crafted interactions, and beautiful imagery.
Web design can produce a large surface area of outcomes. Great design balances restraint with invention—drawing from patterns that have stood the test of time while introducing something new. GPT-5.4 has learned this wide spectrum of design approaches and understands many different ways a website can be built.
When prompts are underspecified, models often fall back to high-frequency patterns from the training data. Some of these are proven conventions, but many are simply overrepresented habits we want to avoid. The result is usually plausible and functional, but it can drift toward generic structure, weak visual hierarchy, and design choices that fall short of what we visualize in our heads.
This guide explains practical techniques for steering GPT-5.4 toward crafting the designs you envision.
Model Improvements
While GPT-5.4 improves across a [range of axes](https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5-4/), for front-end work we focused on three practical gains:
- stronger image understanding throughout the design process
- more functionally complete apps and websites
- better use of tools to inspect, test, and verify its own work
Image understanding and tool use
GPT-5.4 was trained to use image search and image generation tools natively, allowing it to incorporate visual reasoning directly into its design process. For best results, instruct the model to first generate a mood board or several visual options before selecting the final assets.
You can guide the model toward strong visual references by explicitly describing the attributes the images should capture (e.g., style, color palette, composition, or mood). You should also include prompt instructions that guide the model to reuse previously generated images, call the image generation tool to create new visuals, or reference specific external images when required.
Default to using any uploaded/pre-generated images. Otherwise use the image generation tool to create visually stunning image artifacts. Do not reference or link to web images unless the user explicitly asks for them.
Functionality improvements
The model was trained to develop more complete and functionally sound apps. Expect the model to be more reliable over long-horizon tasks. Games and complex user experiences you previously thought were impossible are a reality in one or two turns.
Computer Use and Verification
GPT-5.4 is our first mainline model trained for computer use. It can natively navigate interfaces, and combined with tools such as Playwright, it can iteratively inspect its work, validate behavior, and refine implementations—enabling longer, more autonomous development workflows.
Watch our [launch video](https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5-4/?video=1170427106%20) to see these capabilities in action.
Playwright is particularly valuable for front-end development. It allows the model to inspect rendered pages, test multiple viewports, navigate application flows, and detect issues with state or navigation. Providing a Playwright tool or skill significantly improves the likelihood that GPT-5.4 produces polished, functionally complete interfaces. With improved image understanding, it also provides a way for the model to verify its work visually and check that it matches the reference UI if provided.
Practical tips quickstart
If you adopt only a few practices from this document, start with these:
- Select low reasoning level to begin with.
- Define your design system and constraints upfront (i.e., typography, color palette, layout).
- Provide visual references or a mood board (i.e., attach a screenshot) to provide visual guardrails for the model.
-

items.description: Today, the European Commission presented its proposal for EU Inc., a new single set of corporate rules, building the cornerstone and starting point for the EU\'s 28th regime. EU Inc. is an optional, digital-by-default European corporate framework. It will make it easier for businesses to start, operate and grow across the EU – incentivising them to stay in Europe, and encourage those who once looked elsewhere to return.
items.description_zh: 今天，欧盟委员会正式提出"欧盟公司"（EU Inc.）提案，这是一套全新的统一公司规则，将作为欧盟第28套制度的基石与起点。EU Inc. 是一套可选、默认数字化的欧洲公司框架，旨在让企业更便捷地在欧盟范围内创办、运营和壮大——既激励现有企业扎根欧洲，也鼓励那些曾将目光投向别处的企业回归。

items.description: The content discusses the recurring feeling of being overwhelmed by external societal pressures and personal expectations. It emphasizes the impact of social media and constant connectivity on mental health, suggesting that individuals often struggle with feelings of inadequacy. The piece encourages self-reflection, mindfulness, and setting personal boundaries to combat the negative effects of these pressures.

items.description:
Progress is a premium workout planner and tracker designed to help you train with clarity and consistency.

A workout companion that helps you show up, log your training, and see the wins add up.

Create workout plans, start a session in seconds, and record sets, reps, and weight with a clean, distraction-free interface.

Watch your progress build with personal records, charts, and weekly summaries. Track distance-based workouts, and keep your momentum going with widgets and Live Activities.

Whether you’re just getting started or already deep into your routine, Progress keeps things simple and encouraging — so you can move more, feel stronger, and stay consistent.

Privacy: https://prog.ress.co/privacy
Terms of Use: https://prog.ress.co/terms
EULA: https://www.apple.com/legal/internet-services/itunes/dev/stdeula/
items.description_zh:
Progress 是一款高级健身计划制定与追踪工具，旨在帮助你清晰、持续地训练。

它是你的健身伙伴，助你坚持打卡、记录训练，见证每一次进步的累积。

创建训练计划，数秒内开始训练，并通过简洁无干扰的界面记录组数、次数和重量。

通过个人纪录、图表和每周总结，直观追踪你的进步。追踪距离类训练，并借助小组件和实时活动保持动力。

无论你是刚开始健身，还是已有固定训练节奏，Progress 都能让一切变得简单而鼓舞人心——让你动得更多、感觉更强、坚持更久。

items.description:
Offset ID lets you turn your business card into a beautifully branded Apple Wallet pass — instantly.

Upload your card design, and Offset ID automatically extracts your contact details and generates a digital pass that lives in Apple Wallet. Your pass matches your card’s colors and branding, and can be shared instantly via QR code or link.

Perfect for designers, freelancers, founders, and anyone who cares about craft and presentation.

How it works:
• Upload your business card
• Automatically extract contact details
• Add a branded pass to Apple Wallet
• Share your card instantly via QR code and Public Landing Page

Designed for:
• Designers & creatives
• Freelancers & consultants
• Founders & small teams
• Anyone tired of disposable paper cards

Offset ID is privacy-first, lightweight, and built exclusively for Apple platforms.
items.description_zh:
Offset ID 可将您的名片瞬间转化为设计精美的 Apple Wallet 通行证。

上传您的名片设计，Offset ID 会自动提取您的联系信息，并生成一张可存入 Apple Wallet 的数字通行证。该通行证会匹配您名片的色彩与品牌风格，并可通过二维码或链接即时分享。

专为设计师、自由职业者、创始人以及注重工艺与呈现的人士打造。

运作方式：
• 上传您的名片
• 自动提取联系信息
• 将品牌化通行证添加至 Apple Wallet
• 通过二维码和公共落地页即时分享您的名片

适用人群：
• 设计师与创意工作者
• 自由职业者与顾问
• 创始人及小型团队
• 厌倦一次性纸质名片的任何人

Offset ID 以隐私优先、轻量化为设计理念，专为 Apple 平台打造。

items.description: The S.H.I.T Journal presents itself as a social experiment aimed at decentralizing academic evaluation by giving editing power back to the community. The first volume includes an editorial discussing the implications of this approach, and it invites the community to participate in governance proposals. The journal features blind peer review for submissions, allowing top-rated papers to progress through a rankings system from 'Latrine' to 'Septic Tank'. Current research topics include studies on fecal morphology's correlation with acceptance rates in peer reviews and stochastic modeling of bathroom occupancy during peak deadlines. The platform prioritizes academic freedom and provides guidelines for user engagement.
items.description_zh: 《S.H.I.T 期刊》自称是一场社会实验，旨在通过将编辑权交还给社区来实现学术评价的去中心化。首卷包含一篇社论，探讨了这种方法的含义，并邀请社区参与治理提案。该期刊对投稿采用盲审制度，允许评分最高的论文通过从“茅坑”到“化粪池”的排名系统晋级。当前研究课题包括粪便形态与同行评审接受率相关性的研究，以及截止日期高峰期卫生间占用率的随机建模。该平台优先保障学术自由，并提供了用户参与指南。
items.title: S.H.I.T Journal
items.title_zh: S.H.I.T 日志

items.description:
Born not as a simple zodiac symbol,
but as a lived emotion etched into the Chinese spirit.
It is the collective hope for a better life, renewed year after year.
The Ox steadies through earning step by step,
the Tiger charges forward with bold ambition,
the Dragon rises in grandeur and majesty.
Across generations, zodiac culture has leapt beyond tradition,
becoming a spiritual anchor in the everyday lives of the Chinese people.
items.description_zh:
它并非简单的生肖符号，
而是刻在中国人骨子里的生活情感。
是年复一年，对美好生活的共同期盼。
牛，一步一耕耘，踏实；
虎，一往无前，勇猛；
龙，腾云驾雾，气宇轩昂。
生肖文化，跨越代际，超越传统，
成为中国人日常生活中的精神寄托。
items.title: CIGA design Central Tourbillon Watch · Chinese Zodiac Year of the Hors
items.title_zh: CIGA设计·中央陀飞轮腕表·中国生肖马年

items.description:
Every run becomes a receipt you can print and share.
Your distance, pace, heart rate, and rhythm — all neatly laid out in one clean snapshot.

It’s not about flexing stats.
It’s about remembering the moment.
Not about competing,
but about keeping a record of your own rhythm.

One run. One receipt.
A simple way to make movement part of your everyday story.

Run Receipt — where data feels personal.
items.description_zh:
每一次奔跑，都化作一张可打印、可分享的收据。  
你的距离、配速、心率和步频——所有数据，都清晰呈现在一张简洁的快照中。

这不是炫耀数据，

items.description: The article discusses JPEG compression, an image compression algorithm that optimizes image size by exploiting human visual perception. It details the conversion of RGB color space to Y'CbCr to prioritize luminance over chrominance, utilizing chroma subsampling to reduce color detail without significant visual loss. The JPEG process involves transforming image blocks with Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT), quantizing coefficients, and using Huffman coding for efficient storage. Key points include: 1. Luminance vs. chrominance sensitivity in human vision; 2. Techniques like chroma subsampling (4:4:4, 4:2:2, 4:2:0, 4:1:1); 3. DCT's role in image representation, emphasizing low-frequency values; 4. Quantization reduces data, focusing on main image features; 5. Efficient storage through zigzag scanning and Huffman coding.
items.description_zh: 本文探讨了JPEG压缩技术，这是一种通过利用人类视觉感知特性来优化图像尺寸的算法。文章详细介绍了将RGB色彩空间转换为Y'CbCr的过程，以优先处理亮度信息而非色度信息，并采用色度子采样技术在不显著损失视觉质量的前提下减少色彩细节。JPEG处理流程包括：使用离散余弦变换（DCT）对图像块进行变换、量化系数，以及通过霍夫曼编码实现高效存储。关键要点包括：1. 人眼对亮度与色度的敏感度差异；2. 色度子采样技术（4:4:4、4:2:2、4:2:0、4:1:1）；3. DCT在图像表示中的作用（侧重低频数值）；4. 通过量化减少数据量，聚焦图像主要特征；5. 通过Z字形扫描与霍夫曼编码实现高效存储。
items.title: JPEG compression
items.title_zh: JPEG 压缩

items.description: When multiple AI agents need to work on the same monorepo simultaneously, they each need an isolated working copy with fully functional nodemodules. Git worktrees combined with pnpm's global virtual store make this practical: each worktree gets its own checkout and its own nodemodules, but dependencies are shared across all of them through a single content-addressable store on disk.
items.description_zh: 当多个AI代理需要同时在同一个单体仓库（monorepo）中协作时，每个代理都需要一个独立的、具备完整功能node_modules的工作副本。通过将Git工作树（worktrees）与pnpm的全局虚拟存储相结合，这一需求变得切实可行：每个工作树拥有独立的检出目录和node_modules，但所有工作树通过磁盘上单一的内容可寻址存储共享依赖项。
items.title: pnpm + Git Worktrees for Multi-Agent Development | pnpm
items.title_zh: pnpm + Git Worktrees 实现多智能体开发 | pnpm

items.description:
Introducing Agent 4 — our fastest, most versatile Agent yet. It's built around a simple idea: you should spend your time creating, not coordinating. Agent 4 takes on the tedious-but-necessary work in the background so you can stay in creative flow and ship production-ready software 10x faster. Because Replit is where software is built, run, and shipped — all in one place — Agent 4 can handle both the complex and the mundane, so you can focus on what's uniquely human: creativity.

Summary

Agent 4 is built on four pillars designed to keep you in creative flow and ship production-ready apps 10X faster.

Design Freely: Generate design variants on an infinite canvas, tweak them visually, and apply the best one directly in your app.

Move Faster: Tackle auth, database, back-end functionality and front-end design all at once with parallel agents, with progress across tasks clearly visible. Once done, tasks can be merged seamlessly into the main app.

Ship Anything: Create mobile and web apps, landing pages, decks, videos and more in the same project, with shared context and design so you can scale efficiently.
items.description_zh:
推出 Agent 4 —— 这是我们迄今为止速度最快、功能最全的 Agent。它的核心理念很简单：你应该把时间花在创造上，而不是协调上。Agent 4 在后台处理那些繁琐但必要的工作，让你能保持创作心流，以 10 倍的速度交付生产级软件。因为 Replit 是集软件构建、运行和交付于一体的平台，Agent 4 既能处理复杂任务，也能搞定日常琐事，让你专注于人类独有的部分：创造力。

摘要

Agent 4 建立在四大支柱之上，旨在让你保持创作心流，并以 10 倍的速度交付生产级应用。

自由设计：在无限画布上生成设计变体，直观调整，并将最佳方案直接应用到你的应用中。

更快推进：通过并行 Agent 同时处理身份验证、数据库、后端功能和前端设计，所有任务的进度一目了然。完成后，任务可无缝合并到主应用中。

交付一切：在同一个项目中创建移动应用和网页应用、落地页、演示文稿、视频等，共享上下文和设计，实现高效扩展。
items.title: Replit — Introducing Replit Agent 4: Built for Creativity | Blog
items.title_zh: Replit — 介绍 Replit Agent 4：为创意而生 | 博客

items.description:
Aaron's Waypoints is a travel journal documenting trips through various destinations. Key trips include:
1. **Jeju, South Korea** (Jan 9-12, 2026): Visits Seongsan Ilchulbong and Jusangjeolli Cliff with admission details.
2. **Hokkaido, Japan** (Dec 23, 2025 - Jan 2, 2026): Includes details about the Hokkaido Rail Pass and visits to Shiroi Koibito Park, Sapporo Clock Tower, Sapporo Beer Museum, and various other attractions.
3. **Osaka, Japan** (Dec 19-31, 2025): Highlights visits to Super Nintendo World and Dotonbori Ferris Wheel.
4. **Western US** (Oct 1-9, 2025): Covers major attractions like Universal Studios Hollywood, Yosemite National Park, and Yellowstone National Park.
5. **Vietnam** (Sep 6-8, 2025): Features Ho Chi Minh City experiences, including a street food tour and visits to historical sites.
6. **Russia** (May 2-6, 2025): Visits in St. Petersburg and Moscow, including cultural landmarks like the Hermitage Museum.
7. **Japan** (Various Dates): Includes visits to Kansai and Greater Kanto regions, featuring iconic cultural experiences and sites.
8. **International Travel**: Mentions trips to countries like New Zealand, Egypt, and Singapore with details of significant activities and transportation.
items.description_zh:
Aaron's Waypoints 是一本记录多段旅程的旅行日志。主要行程包括：
1. **韩国济州岛**（2026年1月9日至12日）：游览城山日出峰和柱状节理带，附门票详情。
2. **日本北海道**（2025年12月23日至2026年1月2日）：包含北海道铁路周游券信息，参观白色恋人公园、札幌钟楼、札幌啤酒博物馆及其他景点。
3. **日本大阪**（2025年12月19日至31日）：重点游览超级任天堂世界和道顿堀摩天轮。
4. **美国西部**（2025年10月1日至9日）：涵盖好莱坞环球影城、优胜美地国家公园和黄石国家公园等主要景点。
5. **越南**（2025年9月6日至8日）：体验胡志明市特色，包括街头美食之旅和历史遗址参观。
6. **俄罗斯**（2025年5月2日至6日）：游览圣彼得堡和莫斯科，参观埃尔米塔日博物馆等文化地标。
7. **日本**（多日期）：涵盖关西和关东地区，体验标志性文化景点。
8. **国际旅行**：提及新西兰、埃及和新加坡等国家，包含重要活动及交通细节。
items.title: Aaron's Waypoints
items.title_zh: **Aaron 的路径点**

items.description: The content outlines a tool for assessing the carbon emissions of private jet users compared to the average person's emissions. It lists top private jet polluters, including individuals and families with fleets of jets, such as Thomas Siebel and the Murdoch family. Users can also calculate their own carbon footprint based on various factors such as short and long-haul flights, car usage, yacht days, diet, and home energy consumption. The tool provides a comparison of the user's annual CO₂ emissions to those of the listed private jet users, quantifying the difference in human years needed to match their emissions.
items.description_zh: 该内容介绍了一款评估私人飞机用户碳排放量的工具，并将其与普通人的碳排放量进行对比。工具列出了顶级私人飞机污染者，包括拥有多架飞机的个人和家族，例如托马斯·西贝尔和默多克家族。用户还可根据短途与长途飞行、汽车使用、游艇使用天数、饮食及家庭能源消耗等因素计算自身碳足迹。该工具将用户年二氧化碳排放量与所列私人飞机用户的排放量进行对比，量化了需要多少人类年才能达到他们的排放水平。
items.title: They Fly. Earth Dies.
items.title_zh: 它们飞翔。地球消亡。

items.description: Qclaw is a versatile AI assistant that integrates seamlessly with WeChat, designed for both Mac and Windows users. It features automatic deployment, zero configuration, and works straight out of the box with over 5000 skills available. Users can issue commands via WeChat to manage tasks like document editing, social media interactions, GitHub project management, academic research, and daily reminders. All data is processed locally for enhanced privacy and security. The setup process involves downloading the software, binding it to WeChat, and beginning to issue commands, all achievable within three minutes.
items.description_zh: Qclaw 是一款多功能 AI 助手，能与微信无缝集成，专为 Mac 和 Windows 用户设计。它支持自动部署、零配置，开箱即用，拥有超过 5000 种技能。用户可通过微信发送指令来管理任务，如文档编辑、社交媒体互动、GitHub 项目管理、学术研究和日常提醒。所有数据均在本地处理，以增强隐私和安全性。设置过程包括下载软件、绑定微信并开始发送指令，全程可在三分钟内完成。
items.title: Qclaw - 随时随地，微信一下，Qclaw帮你搞定一切

items.description:
Pool is a new way to save and organize the things you capture.

We take screenshots to remember – a recipe, a podcast, a product, a place. But they usually disappear in your camera roll. Pool turns your screenshots into structured content you can organize, revisit, and act on.
items.description_zh:
Pool 是一种全新的方式，用于保存和整理你捕捉到的内容。

我们截取屏幕截图来记住——一道菜谱、一期播客、一件商品、一个地点。但它们通常会在你的相册中消失。Pool 能将你的截图转化为结构化的内容，让你可以整理、回顾并采取行动。
items.title: ‎Pool – Capture anything App - App Store

The Death of Issue Tracking

问题追踪的消亡

items.description: Albert Invent offers an innovative operating system tailored for chemists, enhancing research and development through AI integration. With a focus on transforming the R&D landscape, Albert OS centralizes projects and experiments, helping scientists document their work more efficiently. Key benefits include: reduced development time (from months to days), improved speed to market for scientific products (doubling efficiency), and enhanced insights based on a large database of molecular structures. Albert's AI models are specifically designed for chemistry, providing personalized, actionable data. Security and compliance are prioritized, ensuring safe data handling while enabling better experiment management.
items.description_zh: Albert Invent 提供专为化学家打造的创新操作系统，通过人工智能集成提升研发效率。该系统聚焦于变革研发格局，将项目与实验集中管理，帮助科学家更高效地记录工作。核心优势包括：开发周期从数月缩短至数天、科学产品上市速度提升一倍（效率翻倍），以及基于海量分子结构数据库获得更深入的洞察。Albert 的 AI 模型专为化学领域设计，可提供个性化、可操作的实用数据。系统优先保障安全性与合规性，在确保数据安全处理的同时，实现更优质的实验管理。
items.title: Albert Invent

items.description: The "History of Software Design" is an upcoming interactive exhibit that aims to showcase significant design milestones in software development, such as the Xerox Alto and features like "Slide to Unlock." The exhibit intends to "restore" certain interfaces and interactions while focusing on a diverse selection of impactful moments. The complexity of software history necessitates a restoration process involving reverse engineering from low-quality assets, which poses unique challenges for designers. The exhibit highlights the stories and contributions of various designs and designers that have shaped the software medium.
items.description_zh: 《软件设计史》是一个即将推出的互动展览，旨在展示软件开发史上的重要设计里程碑，例如施乐奥托（Xerox Alto）以及“滑动解锁”等功能。该展览计划“复原”特定界面与交互方式，同时聚焦于一系列多元且具有影响力的时刻。软件历史的复杂性要求通过从低质量素材中进行逆向工程来完成复原过程，这为设计师带来了独特的挑战。展览将重点呈现那些塑造了软件媒介的各种设计及其创作者的故事与贡献。
items.title: History of Software Design
items.title_zh: 软件设计史

items.description: Agora came to us looking for a unified design system and visual language that could tie together their many products and offerings. They needed something that felt unmistakably Agora, while still flexible enough to move across a wide range of touchpoints. Our answer was the Aura, a visual connective thread that brings consistency and personality to their entire ecosystem. Alongside the Aura, we also solidified a typographic system and iconography library to help hold the brand together across every touchpoint and expression.
items.description_zh: Agora 找到我们，希望建立一个统一的设计系统和视觉语言，将他们的众多产品和服务串联起来。他们需要一种既具有明确 Agora 特色，又能灵活适配各种触点的设计。我们的答案是 Aura——一条视觉连接线，为整个生态系统带来一致性和个性。除了 Aura，我们还完善了字体系统和图标库，帮助品牌在每一个触点和表达方式中保持统一。
items.title: The Collected Works → Rad Work for Rad People™
items.title_zh: 文集 → 为酷人打造的酷工作™

items.description: At Petspan, we specialize in providing cutting-edge longevity treatments like rapamycin for both cats and dogs. Our prescription-based rapamycin protocol targets aging at the cellular level, helping pets live longer, healthier lives. With Petspan’s longevity approach, we aim to extend not just the lifespan of your pets but also to enhance their overall well-being, ensuring their golden years are vibrant and full of vitality.
items.description_zh: 在Petspan，我们专注于为猫狗提供前沿的长寿治疗方案，例如雷帕霉素。我们的处方级雷帕霉素方案从细胞层面靶向衰老过程，帮助宠物活得更久、更健康。通过Petspan的长寿疗法，我们不仅致力于延长宠物的寿命，更注重提升它们的整体健康水平，确保它们的晚年时光充满活力与生机。
items.title: Rapamycin for Cats and Dogs: Prescription Pet Longevity Solutions for a Healthier, Longer Life
items.title_zh: 雷帕霉素用于猫狗：处方宠物长寿解决方案，助力更健康、更长久的生活

items.description: Markelius Sans is a new typeface inspired by hand-drawn interpretations of Helvetica found in the archives of Folkets Hus at Norra Bantorget in Stockholm, designed by architect Sven Markelius. It features a unique charm that reflects its origins in signage from the 1960s. The typeface offers various styles including regular, italic, medium, semi-bold, and bold, along with a unicase stylistic set and arrow sets. It will be part of a new signage system for Folkets Hus, reopening in 2025. The font is designed by Göran Söderström, Figur, and Léo Guibert, and features diverse character sets suitable for various applications.
items.description_zh: Markelius Sans 是一款全新字体，其灵感源自斯德哥尔摩 Norra Bantorget 的 Folkets Hus 档案馆中收藏的手绘 Helvetica 变体，由建筑师 Sven Markelius 设计。这款字体独具魅力，体现了其源自 20 世纪 60 年代标牌的起源。它提供多种样式，包括常规体、斜体、中等体、半粗体和粗体，并附带一套 unicase 风格集和箭头集。该字体将成为 Folkets Hus 新标识系统的一部分，该场馆将于 2025 年重新开放。字体由 Göran Söderström、Figur 和 Léo Guibert 设计，拥有多样化的字符集，适用于各种应用场景。
items.title: Markelius Sans – Letters from Sweden
items.title_zh: Markelius Sans – 来自瑞典的信函

items.description: Beside is an AI-powered receptionist solution designed to streamline communications for businesses by managing phone calls, texts, and meetings. They recently raised $32 million to enhance their services. Key features include automatic call answering, detailed call reports, and integration with over 7,000 applications. Beside allows customization to meet various business needs, such as providing multiple phone lines, shared team inboxes, and personalized AI interactions. It is available on iOS, Mac, and Android, and targets various industries including real estate, construction, and personal care. Subscription plans start at $29.99/month.
items.description_zh: Beside 是一款由人工智能驱动的接待员解决方案，旨在通过管理电话、短信和会议来简化企业的沟通流程。该公司近期筹集了3200万美元，用于提升其服务。其主要功能包括自动接听电话、生成详细的通话报告，以及与超过7000款应用程序集成。Beside 支持定制化设置，以满足不同业务需求，例如提供多条电话线路、共享团队收件箱以及个性化的人工智能交互。该应用可在 iOS、Mac 和 Android 平台上使用，并面向房地产、建筑和个人护理等多个行业。订阅计划起价为每月29.99美元。
items.title: Beside

items.description: Phoenix-4 is the first real-time model to generate and control emotional states, active listening behavior, and continuous facial motion as a single, unified system. It is a real-time behavior generation engine, built from the ground up, that goes beyond photorealism to transform conversation data into emotionally responsive, context-aware facial expression and head motion with millisecond-level latency.
items.description_zh: Phoenix-4 是首个能够以统一系统实时生成并控制情绪状态、主动倾听行为及连续面部动作的模型。这是一款从零构建的实时行为生成引擎，超越照片级真实感，将对话数据转化为具有毫秒级延迟的情绪响应型、情境感知的面部表情与头部运动。
items.title: Phoenix-4: Real-Time Human Rendering with Emotional Intelligence
items.title_zh: Phoenix-4：具备情感智能的实时人体渲染技术

items.description: The content features a collection of creative projects and experiments by Andrew Walz, including a mic that identifies songs on the street, a random route generator, and initiatives like tracking San Francisco parking cops and celebrity Spotify playlists. It also highlights a blog section that reviews the years 2021-2024, discusses various topics like unclaimed property, random jobs, and notable events such as a fake candidate for Congress and wildfires. Each project and blog entry includes links for further exploration.

items.description: Sophie Seita is an artist and researcher whose work explores language as a sensory and conceptual medium through various forms such as performances, publications, sound pieces, textile projects, and workshops. Based in London, her practice has numerous past exhibitions and residencies across the globe. As a lecturer at Goldsmiths, she emphasizes collaboration and non-hierarchical learning. Recent publications include 'Lessons of Decal' and 'My Little Enlightenment Plays.' Seita co-manages The Hildegard von Bingen Society for Gardening Companions, focusing on speculative art and community-driven research. She aims to examine the intersections of art, ecology, political inquiry, and queer experiences through her interdisciplinary approach.
items.description_zh: Sophie Seita是一位艺术家兼研究者，其创作通过表演、出版物、声音作品、纺织项目及工作坊等多种形式，探索语言作为感官与概念媒介的可能性。她常驻伦敦，曾在全球多地举办展览并参与驻留项目。作为金史密斯学院的讲师，她强调协作与非等级制的学习模式。近期出版作品包括《Decal的课程》与《我的小小启蒙剧》。Seita联合管理着"希尔德加德·冯·宾根园艺伙伴协会"，专注于思辨艺术与社群驱动型研究。她致力于通过跨学科方法，审视艺术、生态、政治探究与酷儿体验之间的交汇点。
items.title: Sophie Seita
items.title_zh: Sophie Seita

items.description: We're super excited to finally launch Psylo, a new kind of private web browser for iOS and iPadOS. In Psylo, each tab is its own *silo* with isolated storage, cookies, and even its own IP address. Psylo introduces advanced anti-tracking and anti-fingerprinting features that go beyond what a VPN can offer, thanks to the deep integration between Psylo and our own Mysk Private Proxy Network. Psylo is backed by years of insights from our privacy research at Mysk. Psylo is available today on the App Store as a monthly or annual subscription with a free 7-day trial.
items.description_zh: 我们非常激动地宣布，Psylo 正式上线——这是一款专为 iOS 和 iPadOS 打造的新型隐私网络浏览器。在 Psylo 中，每个标签页都是一个独立的 *隔离仓*，拥有独立的存储空间、Cookie，甚至独立的 IP 地址。得益于 Psylo 与我们自有的 Mysk 私有代理网络之间的深度集成，Psylo 引入了超越 VPN 的先进反追踪和反指纹识别功能。Psylo 基于我们在 Mysk 多年隐私研究的洞察成果。即日起，Psylo 已在 App Store 上架，提供按月或按年订阅，并附带 7 天免费试用。
items.title: Introducing Psylo — A New Kind of Private Web Browser | Mysk Blog – In-Depth Cybersecurity & Mobile App Privacy Research
items.title_zh: 介绍 Psylo —— 一种新型隐私网络浏览器 | Mysk 博客 —— 深度网络安全与移动应用隐私研究

items.description: A technical teardown of a 1-click RCE against OpenClaw (formerly Moltbot/ClawdBot), a viral open-source AI assistant trusted by 100,000+ developers with high-privilege access. See how a settings logic flaw and a WebSocket pivot turn a single webpage visit into token exfiltration, safety-control bypass, and arbitrary command execution.
items.description_zh: 对 OpenClaw（原名 Moltbot/ClawdBot）的一次技术拆解：一次一键 RCE 攻击。OpenClaw 是一款被超过 10 万开发者信任、拥有高权限访问的开源 AI 助手。本文揭示如何通过一个设置逻辑漏洞和 WebSocket 跳板，将一次网页访问转化为令牌窃取、安全控制绕过和任意命令执行。
items.title: depthfirst | 1-Click RCE To Steal Your Moltbot Data and Keys
items.title_zh: depthfirst | 一键远程代码执行，窃取你的 Moltbot 数据和密钥

items.article_text:
ぜんぶそこから始まる。
妻の荒れた手を治したい。その想いから、創業者・鈴木忍は、
z 独学でハンドクリームをつくりました。
目の前を見つめる。寄り添う。相手のために動く。
この「ケア」という考え方が、私たちの根底に息づいています。
ここに並んでいるのは、今、POLAが取り組んでいるケアの一部です。
どのケアも、現場から生まれました。
悲しみ苦しむ人を目の前にしたとき、自分自身がその立場におかれたとき。
そのとき、その人が感じたことが、ケアの出発点になるのです。
気づきを持って、心の声に従って、すべてを仕事に昇華する。
私のために、ではなく、誰かのために働く。
それこそが最も「私らしい」働き方になる。
POLAはそう考えています。
世界を変えるのは、政治や革命だけじゃない。
小さなケアの積み重ねだと思う。飾るのではなく、愚直に。
抽象的ではなく、具体的に。目の前を放っておけない。
もっと、よくしたい。その先の世界は、きっと美しいから。
We Care More. それが、私たちの約束です。
We Care More.
世界を変える、心づかいを。
pola
-
エコバッグをケアする。
岡山の全店舗で、エコバッグ利用とパッケージ回収の促進活動を行う。 -
働き方をケアする。
POLA全社でリモートワークや育休取得など、柔軟な働き方を推進。 -
容器をケアする。
デザインと環境の両立に配慮した、容器設計や販売体制に取り組む。 -
伝統文化をケアする。
広島・熊野筆との共創など、伝統文化を通じた地域活性化を行う。 -
被災地をケアする。
宮城のPOLAは、被災地支援で結びついた団体のイベントに参加。 -
青森県をケアする。
青森のPOLAは、県主催の交流会へ参加し、地元で働く魅力を発信。 -
お母さんをケアする。
長野の沼田さんは、難病児を抱える母親に無償ハンドエステを提供。 -
子どもをケアする。
飛騨高山のPOLAは、子どもたちに地元企業での仕事体験を提供。 -
障がい者をケアする。
北海道の本多さんは、自身の難病経験から、店舗をバリアフリー化。 -
海洋汚染をケアする。
天然由来素材への転換を進め、マイクロプラスチック問題解決に貢献。 -
高齢労働者をケアする。
約1.3万人のシニアがPOLAで働き、やりがいのある職場環境を提供。 -
コロナ禍をケアする。

items.description: Why AI memory should be treated as a dynamic reasoning task rather than static storage, & how logical reasoning enables superhuman capability in this dimension.
items.description_zh:
## 为何AI记忆应被视为动态推理任务而非静态存储，以及逻辑推理如何在此维度实现超人类能力

传统上，AI记忆被设计为静态存储系统——信息被编码、索引并检索，类似于数据库查询。然而，这种范式存在根本性缺陷：它假设记忆是固定的事实集合，而非动态的推理过程。实际上，有效的AI记忆必须是一个持续的推理任务，原因如下：

**1. 记忆的上下文依赖性**  
静态存储无法捕捉记忆的语义上下文。例如，当AI回忆“用户偏好”时，这些偏好可能随对话情境、时间推移或任务目标而变化。动态推理允许AI根据当前上下文重新评估记忆的相关性，而非机械地返回原始数据。

**2. 记忆的推断与补全**  
人类记忆并非完美记录，而是通过推理重构的。AI同样需要从碎片化信息中推断缺失部分。例如，若用户曾提及“喜欢科幻电影”，但未指定具体作品，AI应通过逻辑推理（如“用户喜欢《沙丘》→可能也喜欢《基地》”）来扩展记忆，而非仅存储原始陈述。

**3. 记忆的冲突解决**  
静态存储无法处理矛盾信息。当新信息与旧记忆冲突时（如用户改变偏好），AI需通过逻辑推理判断优先级（如“近期行为比早期陈述更可靠”），而非简单覆盖或保留所有数据。

**4. 记忆的抽象与泛化**  
动态推理使AI能从具体实例中提取抽象规则。例如，从多次交互中归纳出“用户倾向于在压力下选择简单方案”，而非存储每个具体决策。这种泛化能力是超人类记忆的关键。

### 逻辑推理如何实现超人类能力

逻辑推理将AI记忆从被动存储转变为主动认知工具，具体体现在：

- **因果链构建**：通过演绎推理，AI能连接看似无关的记忆片段，形成因果解释。例如，从“用户上周取消订阅”和“用户抱怨价格”推断出“价格敏感度是核心因素”，从而预测未来行为。

- **反事实推理**：逻辑推理允许AI评估“如果……会怎样”的场景，从而优化记忆的实用性。例如，“如果用户当时获得折扣，是否会继续订阅？”这种推理使记忆不仅记录过去，还能指导未来策略。

- **元记忆管理**：AI可通过推理决定哪些记忆应被保留、合并或遗忘。例如，基于“信息冗余度”和“使用频率”的逻辑规则，自动压缩低价值记忆，释放资源用于高价值推理。

- **跨模态推理**：当记忆涉及文本、图像、语音等多模态数据时，逻辑推理能建立跨模态关联。例如，从用户语音语调（紧张）与文本内容（犹豫）推理出“用户对当前方案缺乏信心”，从而调整回应策略。

### 结论

将AI记忆视为动态推理任务，而非静态存储，是迈向超人类智能的关键一步。逻辑推理使AI不仅能“记住”，更能“理解”记忆的深层含义，在冲突、模糊和变化中保持一致性。这种能力超越了人类记忆的局限性——人类受限于生物遗忘曲线和认知偏差，而AI通过结构化推理可实现精确、可扩展且自适应的记忆系统。最终，记忆不再是过去的仓库，而是未来的推理引擎。
items.title: Memory as Reasoning
items.title_zh: 记忆即推理
